frontmatter.title: Add books to the Airtable form
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for submitting a selected Book of the Week title through Airtable so it is ready for website follow-up.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Book of the week events/Add books to the Airtable form.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Add a Book of the Week book record through the Airtable form.
- Outcome: The book is submitted to the database and ready for the follow-up website script.
- Trigger: A book has been selected for Book of the Week and the author/person record exists.
- Frequency: Whenever a new Book of the Week book is added.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Book of the Week Airtable form and the schedule spreadsheet.
- Tools: Airtable form, publisher or book website, schedule spreadsheet.
- Inputs: Author email, title, description, event start date, book page, cover URL or cover file, publisher page, and optional Amazon/GitHub links.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you need to do is fill up the "Author's email"

    Note: Adding the [“person” form](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1GsoZjajThPDXm2Nnb98k79Fzg3JSI9cL/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=117889684806099286252&rtpof=true&sd=true) is a prerequisite.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for fill up the "Author's email". Look for the red box or arrow around "Author's email", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next is to add the "Title" and "Description" of the book.

    Note: “Titles” and “Descriptions” of the book can be seen on the publisher's website. You just copy them directly from the website. Normally, the description of the book can be found under the "About the book" column of the website.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for add the "Title" and "Description" of the book. Look for the red boxes or arrows around "Title", "Description", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, select the "Date" of the event.

    Note: It must be the Monday of the week when the event happens.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 3 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for select the "Date" of the event. Look for the red box or arrow around "Date", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And then proceed publisher's website for the “Book cover URL”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 4 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for proceed publisher's website for the "Book cover URL". Look for the red box or arrow around "Book cover URL", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
To copy the book cover URL, right-click on the image or the book cover and select
    "Copy Image Address"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for to copy the book cover URL, right click on the image or the book cover and select "Copy Image Address". Look for the red box or arrow around "Copy Image Address", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then paste it below the "Book cover URL"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 6 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for then paste it below the "Book cover URL". Look for the red box or arrow around "Book cover URL", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: If there's no cover on the page, you can upload the cover by clicking "Attach file"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 7 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for if there's no cover on the page, you can upload the cover by clicking "Attach file". Look for the red box or arrow around "Attach file", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Drag the book cover from your computer.
    Note: If the size of the cover is above 300 pixels in width and height, resize it and save it as .jpg.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 8 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for drag the book cover from your computer. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Afterward, paste the book website URL.

    Note: Book websites are dedicated websites for the book. You may ask for the book page URL from the authors. If none, just leave it blank.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 9 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for paste the book website URL. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Don't forget to paste the publisher's page.

    Note: There is no publisher's page for self-published books. If there is a publisher, you can copy the URL of the book from the publsiher itself. Publishers include: Packt, Manning, O’Reilly.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 10 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for don't forget to paste the publisher's page. Look for the red box or arrow around Publish, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
The next thing you will be doing is paste the URL of the book's page from Amazon.

    Note: If the book can't be found on amazon, just leave it blank.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 11 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for the next thing you will be doing is paste the URL of the book's page from Amazon. Look for the red box or arrow around Next, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next, paste the book's Github repo.

    Note: You may search the GitHub repo through Google or you may ask the author for the link.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 12 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for paste the book's Github repo. Look for the red box or arrow around Next, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After reviewing your work, click “Submit”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 13 of the Add books to the Airtable form process by showing the screen for after reviewing your work, click "Submit". Look for the red box or arrow around "Submit", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

prose.body_md:
How to add books to the Airtable forms
This procedure will show you the steps on how to add books to the Airtable forms.

Step-by-step Instructions

flat_steps.body_md: And then, go to "\_books"

flat_steps.body_md:
Under "authors:". add the name of the new author and in the "description" add also

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 6 of the Adding an author to book of the week pages process by showing the screen for after adding, click "Commit changes". Look for the red box or arrow around "Commit changes", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

frontmatter.source: Processes/Book of the week events/Have a first contact with the author_.docx

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access:
- Tools:

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you will be doing is email the book author and propose the tentative
    the date that is suitable for the book-of-the-week event.
    Note: You can navigate the dates by going to the google spreadsheet: [DataTalks.Club schedule](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit?usp=drive_link)

    When proposing a date for the next Book of the Week event, ensure there is at least a one-week gap after the previous event.

    Example:

    If Machine Learning Algorithms in Depth is scheduled for September 08–12, the next available slot should be September 22–26.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Have a first contact with the author process by showing the screen for the first thing you will be doing is email the book author and propose the tentative the date that is suitable for. Look for the red box or arrow around Next, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

Look for the red box or arrow around "proposed date", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

frontmatter.title: How to schedule the announcement in Slack
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for scheduling Book of the Week announcements in Slack and forwarding them to the announcements channel.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Book of the week events/How to schedule the announcement in Slack.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Schedule the Book of the Week announcement in Slack.
- Outcome: The announcement is scheduled in `#book-of-the-week` and forwarded to `#announcements`.
- Trigger: A Book of the Week event is ready to announce.
- Frequency: Weekly for Book of the Week events.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Slack workspace with permission to schedule and forward messages.
- Tools: Slack, Book of the Week announcement template.
- Inputs: Book title, author name, event discussion details, and book cover image.

flat_steps.body_md:
First, copy and paste the previous \#book-of-the-week event announcement.

    Note: This will serve as the template for the announcement.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the How to schedule the announcement in Slack process by showing the screen for first, copy and paste the previous \ book of the week event announcement. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Next, edit the description of the announcement. This includes the name and author of the book. [Template](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1hb2dY0f4iSDiR9rPJPI-Glfl8XEDldUvzH1J-lTOcTQ/edit?usp=sharing)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the How to schedule the announcement in Slack process by showing the screen for edit the description of the announcement. This includes the name and author of the book. Template. Look for the red box or arrow around Next, Edit, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After editing the description, add the picture of the book by clicking on the plus icon and selecting "Upload from your computer"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 3 of the How to schedule the announcement in Slack process by showing the screen for after editing the description, add the picture of the book by clicking on the plus icon and selecting "Upload from. Look for the red box or arrow around "Upload from your computer", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And then choose the picture of the book and click "Open"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 4 of the How to schedule the announcement in Slack process by showing the screen for choose the picture of the book and click "Open". Look for the red box or arrow around "Open", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Once the picture of the book is attached, hover and click the dropdown button and click “Custom time”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the How to schedule the announcement in Slack process by showing the screen for once the picture of the book is attached, hover and click the dropdown button and click "Custom time". Look for the red box or arrow around "Custom time", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, select the scheduled date of the book to be announced and click "Schedule message"

    Note: The schedule of the announcement should be at 7:30 CET.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 6 of the How to schedule the announcement in Slack process by showing the screen for select the scheduled date of the book to be announced and click "Schedule message". Look for the red box or arrow around "Schedule message", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After posting in the \#book-of-the-week, repost it to \#announcements.
    Hover over the message you sent until icons appear on the right, then click the ‘Forward message’ icon.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 7 of the How to schedule the announcement in Slack process by showing the screen for after posting in the \ book of the week, repost it to \ announcements. Hover over the message you sent until icons. Look for the red box or arrow around Forward, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Forward the message to \#announcements and use the following text:

    “Join \#book-of-the-week and ask @author anything!”

    Then click on the “Forward” button.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 8 of the How to schedule the announcement in Slack process by showing the screen for forward the message to \ announcements and use the following text: "Join \ book of the week and ask @author. Look for the red boxes or arrows around "Join \ book of the week and ask @author anything!", "Forward", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

prose.body_md:
How to schedule the announcement in Slack
This procedure will show you the steps on how to schedule the announcement in Slack.

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Invite people to Slack from the Airtable form

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Manually invite people to the DataTalks.Club Slack when the normal invite link did not work.
- Outcome: The person receives a Slack invitation or gets a response explaining that they are already registered.
- Trigger: Someone emails that they cannot join Slack or did not receive the invite.
- Frequency: Whenever Slack invite problems are reported.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club Slack workspace and the requester email.
- Tools: Slack invite flow and email.
- Inputs: Requester email address and any error or context from their message.

Club". Look for the red box or arrow around "Invite people to DataTalks. Club", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Select book of the week winners
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for selecting Book of the Week giveaway winners, announcing them in Slack, and collecting winner emails.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Book of the week events/Select book of the week winners.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Select and announce Book of the Week giveaway winners.
- Outcome: Winners are chosen or confirmed, announced in Slack, and asked to send their emails.
- Trigger: Before the Friday winner announcement for a Book of the Week event.
- Frequency: Weekly for Book of the Week giveaways.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Slack and the Book of the Week email tracking spreadsheet.
- Tools: Slack, random.org List Randomizer, text editor, Book of the Week announcement template.
- Inputs: Participant names, author preference for random or author-selected winners, book title, author, and publisher.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you need to do is copy the names on the slack community

    Note: If someone asked to keep them out of the book of the week winners, don’t include them on the randomizer.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for copy the names on the slack community. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
(Step 2 to 6 are not included) Next is to paste the names on a list.

    Note: In this example, the names are pasted in the Visual Studio code. You may also use notepad to paste the names temporarily. Moreover, edit some unnecessary text included beside the name.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for (Step 2 to 6 are not included) Next is to paste the names on a list. Look for the red box or arrow around Next, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After which, copy the list you pasted in the Visual Studio code and paste it on Random. Org’s “List Randomizer”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 3 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for after which, copy the list you pasted in the Visual Studio code and paste it on Random. Org's "List Randomizer". Look for the red box or arrow around "List Randomizer", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click “randomize”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 4 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for click "randomize". Look for the red box or arrow around "randomize", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, copy the names that are randomized.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for , copy the names that are randomized. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, paste the first five names that are randomized.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 6 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for paste the first five names that are randomized. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: The next thing to do is copy the announcement format from the previous dates. [Template](https://docs.google.com/document/d/10SYeHAEvf7wt026t93dSD0ZtQZ82g0qMOQwodTTQvbM/edit?usp=sharing).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 7 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for the next thing to do is copy the announcement format from the previous dates. Template. Look for the red box or arrow around Next, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next is to paste the announcement you copied earlier together with the 5 lucky winner names and edit the name of the author and the publisher.

    Note: Don’t forget to add “@” before the name of the winners.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 8 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for paste the announcement you copied earlier together with the 5 lucky winner names and edit the name of the author. Look for the red box or arrow around Next, Publish, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Afterward, copy the edited announcement.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 9 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for copy the edited announcement. Look for the red box or arrow around Edit, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Lastly, paste the announcement into the slack community and click the send icon below.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 10 of the Select book of the week winners process by showing the screen for paste the announcement into the slack community and click the send icon below. Look for the red box or arrow around Send, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

prose.body_md:
How to select book of the week winners
This procedure will show you the steps on how to select book of the week winners.

Step-by-step Instructions

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Outcome: The confirmed user account is deactivated in Slack.
- Trigger: A specific Slack user needs to be banned for unsolicited promotion.
- Frequency: As needed.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Slack admin access to manage members.
- Tools: Slack workspace administration.
- Inputs: User name and profile details to confirm the correct account.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is click the dropdown list beside “DataTalksClub” hover your mouse to “Settings and Administration” and click “Manage members”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click the dropdown list beside “DataTalksClub” hover your mouse to “Settings and Administration” and click “Manage members” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “DataTalksClub” and “Settings and Administration”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, search for the name of the user in the search field.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about search for the name of the user in the search field so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click the three-dotted button and select “Deactivate account”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click the three-dotted button and select “Deactivate account” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Deactivate account”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Lastly, select “Deactivate”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to select “Deactivate” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Deactivate”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Export Create a dump of Slack Data
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for exporting a monthly Slack data dump for community analytics and archival access.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Slack/Export_Create a dump of Slack Data.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Downloading all the data from Slack
- Outcome: We perform monthly data dumps so that the community members can access our Slack data for their analytics and other purposes.
- Trigger: When the monthly reminder is triggered or per request when we need to generate a data dump.
- Frequency: Monthly, or as requested.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Slack workspace settings and export page.
- Tools: Slack workspace administration and email notification.
- Inputs: Export date range and destination for the downloaded dump.

steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open Slack and select “DataTalksClub” on the upper left.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open Slack and select “DataTalksClub” on the upper left so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “DataTalksClub”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Then, click “Tools and Settings” and select “Workplace settings”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click “Tools and Settings” and select “Workplace settings” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Tools and Settings” and “Workplace settings”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Next, click “Import/Export Data”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click “Import/Export Data” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Import/Export Data”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Then, click “Export”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click “Export” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Export”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: On the ‘Export Data Range”, click the dropdown button and select “Entire History”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about on the ‘Export Data Range”, click the dropdown button and select “Entire History” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Entire History”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md:
Then, click “Start Export”

    Note: Exporting will usually take around 5-10 minutes to complete.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click “Start Export” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Start Export”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md:
Once the export is done, an email from slack will send you a notification. Click “Visit your workplace’s export page”


    You will also get a notification from Slack bot. You can click on the link there too – both links lead to the samepage

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about once the export is done, an email from slack will send you a notification. Click “Visit your workplace’s export page” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Visit your workplace’s export page”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

This screenshot anchors the step about you will also get a notification from Slack bot. You can click on the link there too – both links lead to the samepage so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: On the new page, scroll down, and on the “Past Exports” click the file to be downloaded under “Status”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about on the new page, scroll down, and on the “Past Exports” click the file to be downloaded under “Status” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Past Exports” and “Status”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md:
Once the file has been downloaded

    - open the [“#slack-dump” channel](https://app.slack.com/client/T01ATQK62F8/C042X0VTADR)
    - upload the downloaded file on the channel (drag-and-drop it there)
    - use the text “Fresh slack dump with all the data! 📢”
    - click the send icon

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click the send icon so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the reporting value or action control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md:
After posting the dump on the special channel, repost it to \#general.

    To do this, hover over the message you sent until icons appear on the right, then click the ‘Forward message’ icon.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about to do this, hover over the message you sent until icons appear on the right, then click the ‘Forward message’ icon so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the reporting value or action control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md:
And then, use the following text
    📢 You can find the fresh Slack dump in \#slack-dump!

    add “#announcements” as the channel and click “Forward”

    Loom links:

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to add “#announcements” as the channel and click “Forward” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “#announcements” and “Forward”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

frontmatter.summary: Procedure for reactivating a deactivated DataTalks.Club Slack member account through Slack admin tools.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Slack/Reactivate a Slack account.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Reactivate a deactivated Slack member account.
- Outcome: The selected member account is active again in Slack.
- Trigger: A member needs their Slack account restored.
- Frequency: As needed.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Slack admin access to manage members.
- Tools: Slack workspace administration.
- Inputs: Member email address or account details.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open DataTalks.Club slack community and on the top left side of your screen, select “DataTalks.Club”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open DataTalks.Club slack community and on the top left side of your screen, select “DataTalks.Club” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “DataTalks.Club”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And then select “Settings & administration”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to select “Settings & administration” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Settings & administration”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Creating Course Databases in Airtable
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for creating an Airtable database to collect registrations and manage setup for a new course cohort.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Courses/Creating Course Databases in Airtable.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: The process involves creating a database for a new course.
- Outcome: To collect registrations from the students of the course.
- Trigger: Before the course begins, for registration and updates.
- Frequency: Once per course or cohort setup.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Airtable template base.
- Tools: Airtable.
- Inputs: Course name, form title, sponsor fields, and branding preferences.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you need to do is go to the [Airtable](https://airtable.com/) and find the “\[TEMPLATE\] Zoomcamp”

    Note: The template can sometimes be located at the top but may also appear anywhere at the bottom.

screenshots.caption: This Airtable home screen highlights the `[TEMPLATE] Zoomcamp` base. Start from this template so the new course inherits the expected views, form, and fields.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on the three dots, and in the pop-up box, select “Duplicate base.”

screenshots.caption: This base menu highlights Duplicate base. Use this action to create a course-specific copy without changing the shared template.

flat_steps.body_md:
Replace the template name by typing the name of the course. Then uncheck or turn off the "Duplicate records" option. Finally, click “Duplicate base.”

    Note: In this example the name of the course is , “Open-Source LLM Zoomcamp.”

screenshots.caption: This duplicate dialog shows the new course name field and the Duplicate records toggle. Turn records off before duplicating so the new course starts with empty registration data.

flat_steps.body_md: After duplicating, click on “Open base”.

screenshots.caption: This success dialog confirms the base was duplicated and offers Open base. Open it immediately to continue configuring the form and fields in the new course base.

flat_steps.body_md: On the sidebar, click on the form “TODO: TITLE”.

screenshots.caption: This shows the copied base sidebar with the placeholder form title. Select the TODO form so the public registration page can be renamed for the course.

flat_steps.body_md: Update the name of the title.

screenshots.caption: This form editor shows the course title replacing the placeholder. Verify the title students will see before adjusting the rest of the form.

flat_steps.body_md:
Scroll down.

    Note: You might need to change other settings by toggling options as per instructions, but in this example, no changes are required.

screenshots.caption: This form settings area shows follow-up and branding options. Check these settings while scrolling so the copied template behavior still matches the course requirements.

flat_steps.body_md:
There are cases where additional data is required. In this case, the course is sponsored, and the sponsor has requested the emails of those who signed up for the course.

    On the side bar, select the “Grid View”.

screenshots.caption: This shows the Grid View selected from the sidebar. Switch here when you need to add database fields that support the registration form.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on the “+” sign, then select the “Checkbox”.

screenshots.caption: This field type menu highlights Checkbox. Choose it for sponsor email consent because the form needs a clear yes/no response.

flat_steps.body_md: Type “Sponsor share email” in the “Field name (optional)”. Then click on “Create Field”.

screenshots.caption: This create-field dialog shows `Sponsor share email` as a checkbox field. Confirm the field name and type before creating it so the sponsor consent field appears in the base.

flat_steps.body_md:
Go to the side bar and select the form “Open-Source LLM Zoomcamp”.

    In the “Fields” click the field we just created and drag it above the Blue submit button.

screenshots.caption: This form editor shows the new sponsor field being positioned before the submit button. Drag it into the visible form section so registrants answer it before submitting.

flat_steps.body_md: Rename the Field name with “Share my email with the course sponsor”

screenshots.caption: This field editor shows the public-facing consent label and helper text. Verify the wording makes the sponsor-sharing decision explicit to registrants.

flat_steps.body_md: Lastly, go back to the Home page, click on the three dots of the database we created, and select “Customize appearance.”

screenshots.caption: This Airtable base menu highlights Customize appearance. Use it after setup so the new base has a recognizable course color in the workspace.

flat_steps.body_md: Select the light pink color.

screenshots.caption: This color picker shows the appearance palette for the course base. Select the intended color so the course base is easy to distinguish from templates and other cohorts.

prose.body_md:
Creating Course Databases in Airtable
This document shows the steps to creating Course Databases in Airtable.

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Pulling contacts from Airtable form to Mailchip for Courses
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for transferring course registration contacts from Airtable forms to Mailchimp for course email campaigns.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Airtable/Pulling contacts from Airtable form to Mailchip for Courses.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: The process involves transferring participant contact information submitted via Airtable forms to MailChimp for email campaigns
- Outcome: To streamline communication and automation of course-related emails to participants
- Trigger: Before the course begins for registration and updates
- Frequency: Once per course launch or cohort setup.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Mailchimp, Airtable, and the MailChimpPoller GitHub repository.
- Tools: Mailchimp, Airtable, GitHub Actions.
- Inputs: Course tag, Airtable form/view details, and welcome email content.

steps.body_md: Go to [https://us19.admin.mailchimp.com/audience/tags/](https://us19.admin.mailchimp.com/audience/tags/) and click “Create new tag” on the top right side of the screen.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Mailchimp audience tags page with the Create new tag button. This is where the course-specific tag is created before automation setup.

steps.body_md:
Name the layer using the same format from the past tags and click “create”.

    Note: for example: de-zoomcamp-2024

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the tag creation dialog in Mailchimp. It helps confirm the new tag follows the existing course-year naming pattern before you create it.

steps.body_md:
Hover over the tab on the left, select “All journeys” and click “Classic Automations”.


    ##

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Mailchimp navigation path to Classic Automations. This is the section where existing course email automations can be reused.

steps.body_md: Find the past automation for the course and select “Replicate”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows a previous course automation with the Replicate option. Replicating keeps the campaign structure while letting you update it for the new course.

steps.body_md: It would bring you to a new page and click “Edit Settings” below the course name.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the replicated automation page with Edit Settings under the course name. This is where the workflow name and course metadata are adjusted.

steps.body_md: Under Workflow name, edit the year to the year of the course you are working on and click “Save.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Mailchimp workflow name field and Save control. Updating the year here keeps the replicated automation aligned with the current course cohort.

steps.body_md: It would bring you the the default page and click “Edit”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the automation overview with the Edit button. Opening it lets you update the trigger settings and email content for the new tag.

steps.body_md: Select the right tag under “Settings” and click “Save”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the automation settings where the audience tag is selected. Choosing the new course tag ensures only the intended Airtable contacts enter the campaign.

steps.body_md:
After, click “Design Email". ![](../../../images/airtable/pulling-contacts-from-airtable-form-to-mailchip-for-courses/media/image14.jpg)
    Image note: The screenshot shows the Design Email control in the Mailchimp automation. This opens the email content editor where links and copy are checked.
Check if all links are correct and update if necessary.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Mailchimp email editor with linked content. It highlights where course links need to be reviewed and corrected.

steps.body_md:
For “Watch here”, change it to “Sign up here” and change the link to the Luma URL of the event.

    Note: After the live stream, update the registration link with the YouTube recording link and change back to “Watch here”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the email button text and link settings. Change the button to Sign up here and point it to the Luma registration URL before launch.

steps.body_md: Select “Next” and click “Save and Continue”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the final Mailchimp editor controls for Next and Save and Continue. These save the email edits and return you to the automation flow.

steps.body_md: Go to [https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/airtable-mailchimp-poller](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/airtable-mailchimp-poller) and select “config.yaml”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the airtable-mailchimp-poller repository with config.yaml selected. This file controls which Airtable tags the poller syncs to Mailchimp.

steps.body_md: Find the appropriate tag and update the year.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the config.yaml tag entry that needs a year update. Matching this value to the Mailchimp tag keeps the poller and automation connected.

steps.body_md: Select “Actions” and Click “Run Workflow”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the GitHub Actions page with Run Workflow. Running it triggers the Airtable-to-Mailchimp sync after the configuration update.

steps.body_md: After running the workflow, go back to Mailchimp and now you can click “View Queue” to verify that emails are being pulled properly.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows Mailchimp's View Queue control for the automation. The queue confirms whether contacts from Airtable are being added to the email flow.

frontmatter.title: Creating a Calendar in the Course
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for creating public Google Calendar events for course sessions and office hours.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Courses/Creating a Calendar in the Course.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Create public course calendar events for course sessions.
- Outcome: Course participants see the event on the correct course calendar.
- Trigger: A course event or office-hours session is scheduled.
- Frequency: Per course event.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Course Google Calendar.
- Tools: Google Calendar, YouTube.
- Inputs: Event title, date, time, course calendar, and public stream link.

flat_steps.body_md:
First, select the “Data Engineering Zoomcamp” calendar

    Note: Don’t add any guests.

screenshots.caption: This shows the calendar dropdown with the course calendar selected and no guest entry required. Verify the event is assigned to the course calendar rather than a personal or unrelated calendar.

flat_steps.body_md: After, change the title of the zoom calendar.

screenshots.caption: This shows the event title field updated to the session name and the YouTube channel in the location field. Use it to check that participants will see the correct public title before saving.

flat_steps.body_md: On the location, add the link to our YouTube channel and the notes. Once done, click “Save”

screenshots.caption: This confirms both the YouTube channel link in Location and the streaming note in Description. Check these fields together so calendar subscribers know where the session will be streamed.

frontmatter.title: Backing Up Data for Disabled Course
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for backing up Airtable records after disabling a course Mailchimp and Airtable integration.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Courses/Backing Up Data for Disabled Course.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: backing up data after disabling the MailChimp+Airtable Integration.
- Outcome: Processed Airtable records are exported to Google Drive and removed from the processed view.
- Trigger: A course Mailchimp/Airtable integration has been disabled.
- Frequency: When disabling a course cohort.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Airtable course base and course backup folder in Google Drive.
- Tools: Airtable, Google Drive.
- Inputs: Processed registration records and target backup location.

flat_steps.body_md:
Go to [Airtable](https://airtable.com/). Find and click on the course.
    In this example is the Mlops Zoomcamp Course.

screenshots.caption: This shows the Airtable home screen with the MLOps Zoomcamp base highlighted. Confirm the disabled course base before exporting or deleting any registration records.

flat_steps.body_md: In this process we want to save the registrations and to remove it from the database. So go to “processed”.

screenshots.caption: This shows the course base views and highlights the processed view. Use this view because these records have already received the Mailchimp email and are the ones safe to back up and remove.

flat_steps.body_md: Click the dropdown option beside “processed” and select “Download CSV”.

screenshots.caption: This dropdown shows the Download CSV action for the processed view. Export from this exact view so the backup contains the same records that will later be deleted.

flat_steps.body_md:
Go to [config.yaml](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/airtable-mailchimp-poller/blob/main/config.yaml) in [airtable-mailchimp-poller](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/airtable-mailchimp-poller) in the Github Repository and copy the tag course.
    In this example is “mlops-zoomcamp-2025”

screenshots.caption: This GitHub config view highlights the course tag used by the poller. Copy this tag for the backup filename so the exported CSV matches the disabled integration name.

flat_steps.body_md: Go to the File Manager and rename the downloaded CSV using the copied course tag name.

screenshots.caption: This file manager view shows the freshly downloaded Airtable CSV before renaming. Use it to identify the correct download and avoid renaming an unrelated file.

flat_steps.body_md: To check if the file is complete and records are all in there, click on it.

screenshots.caption: This shows the renamed CSV using the course tag. Confirm the filename matches the poller tag before opening it for record-count validation.

flat_steps.body_md:
Scroll down to the last number.
    Then go back to the [course in Airtable](https://airtable.com/). Scroll down to the last number and check if it matches. In this example we had 8,607.

    Note: There may be a difference of 1 due to the header row.

screenshots.caption: This spreadsheet view shows the bottom row count of the exported CSV. Compare this count with Airtable so you know the backup is complete before deleting processed records.

flat_steps.body_md: Go to [courses](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ptsZ7m1aGTvj7fJLH_BNXjf8bOlGoNPN) in the backup of DTC Files drive.

screenshots.caption: This Google Drive folder shows the course backup location and existing cohort CSVs. Upload the new export here so disabled-course backups stay grouped by course tag and year.

flat_steps.body_md: Upload or drag the file from the File Manager into the Drive.

screenshots.caption: This shows the renamed CSV being placed alongside existing backup files in Drive. Confirm the uploaded file appears in the folder before returning to Airtable for deletion.

flat_steps.body_md: Go back to the [course in Airtable](https://airtable.com/). Click the checkbox on top to select all. Right click on your mouse and select “Delete all selected records”.

screenshots.caption: This Airtable view shows all processed records selected and the delete action highlighted. Use it only after the CSV has been uploaded and validated, because this removes processed registration rows.

flat_steps.body_md:
Click on “Delete” in the pop up window.

    Note: This applies only to people from this table to whom we already sent an email. It’s important that we only do it for this “processed”, because the “unprocessed “ ones have not received an email yet, they are fresh people.

screenshots.caption: This delete confirmation shows the number of records that will be removed. Check that the count matches the processed export before confirming Delete.

prose.body_md:
Backing Up Data for Disabled Course
This document shows the steps to Backing Up Data for Disabled Course.

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Updating the Timetable of the Courses at Data Talks Club
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for updating course timetable information on the DataTalks.Club website through GitHub.

summary.body_md:

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: GitHub access to datatalksclub.github.io.
- Tools: GitHub web editor.
- Inputs: Course post, updated schedule details, and course title.

flat_steps.body_md: First, proceed to the website repository, “datatalksclub,github.io” and navigate through the page and click “posts”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Updating the Timetable of the Courses at Data Talks Club process by showing the screen for first, proceed to the website repository, "datatalksclub,github.io" and navigate through the page and click "posts". Look for the red boxes or arrows around "datatalksclub,github.io", "posts", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Updating the Timetable of the Courses at Data Talks Club process by showing the screen for first, proceed to the website repository, "datatalksclub,github.io" and navigate through the page and click "posts". Look for the red boxes or arrows around "datatalksclub,github.io", "posts", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Then, scroll down and look for the post that you wish to update.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the Updating the Timetable of the Courses at Data Talks Club process by showing the screen for scroll down and look for the post that you wish to update. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

Look for the red box or arrow around "Edit this file", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, navigate through the page and look for the part where you wish to update.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the Updating the Timetable of the Courses at Data Talks Club process by showing the screen for click "Commit changes" to save the changes made.
