frontmatter.title: Sending emails via Luma (feedback for project of the week)
frontmatter.summary: Steps to send a feedback email to Project of the Week participants through Luma, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Luma/Sending emails via Luma (feedback for project of the week).docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Send a feedback email to Project of the Week participants through Luma.
- Outcome: Participants receive a Luma email blast with the feedback form link.
- Trigger: A Project of the Week event has ended.
- Frequency: After each Project of the Week event.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Luma access to the past Project of the Week event.
- Tools: Luma email blast editor, feedback form.
- Inputs: Project name, feedback form link, and feedback email copy.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open Luma and click on “Past” and select the recent Project of the week event.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Past events view in Luma with the recent Project of the Week event. This ensures the feedback email is sent from the completed event, not an upcoming one.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click on “Emails” and select “New Email Blast”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Emails tab and New Email Blast option for the selected Luma event. This opens the editor used to send the feedback request to attendees.

flat_steps.body_md:
Then, add the subject and the text of the email.

    Note:

    Subject format: Feedback: Project of the Week - \<NAME OF PROJECT\>

    Body:

    Hi everyone!

    Thanks a lot for participating in our project of the week about GitHub actions.

    We will keep hosting more projects of the week! So, we would like to get your input.

    Please fill out this feedback form: \<LINK\>. You can fill it out even if you participated for one day!

    It will take ~5 minutes of your time and help improve future projects.

    Thank you!

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the feedback email draft with the subject and body filled in. It provides the expected message structure, including the feedback form link and closing thank-you line.

flat_steps.body_md: Once done, click “Send Email”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Send Email button in Luma's email blast editor. Use it only after confirming the subject, body, and feedback form link are correct.

prose.body_md:
How to Send emails via Luma (feedback for project of the week)
This procedure will show you the steps on how to Send emails via Luma (feedback for project of the week)

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Updating project of the week reminders (emails) in Luma
frontmatter.summary: Steps to replace default Luma reminders with daily Project of the Week reminders, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Luma/Updating project of the week reminders (emails) in Luma.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Replace default Luma reminders with daily Project of the Week reminders.
- Outcome: Each session has one reminder sent at the session start time with the correct day number.
- Trigger: A Project of the Week event series has been created in Luma.
- Frequency: For each session in every Project of the Week series.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Admin access to edit the Project of the Week event series in Luma.
- Tools: Luma scheduled emails.
- Inputs: Event series, session start times, event name, and day numbers.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open Luma and click “events” then click the event you want to edit.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Luma Events view with the project event selected. It helps verify you are editing the correct Project of the Week series.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click “Details”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Details tab inside the Luma event editor. Scheduled reminder settings are managed from this event details area.

flat_steps.body_md: Next, click the drop down list under “Scheduled Emails”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Scheduled Emails section expanded in Luma. This is where the default reminders can be reviewed before editing them.

flat_steps.body_md: By default, Luma creates three reminders. For this kind of event, project of the week event, we don’t to have a 1-hr reminder, 5-minute reminder and the like. We only want to send a reminder during the event. To edit it, click the “pen” icon.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the default reminder list with the edit control for a reminder. It identifies the reminder entry to keep and adjust for the Project of the Week schedule.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, change the subject of the event to this format: “Daily Reminder to join \<EVENT NAME\> (Day \<NUMBER\>.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the reminder subject field in Luma. It demonstrates where to enter the daily reminder subject with the event name and day number.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, change the time of the event. Once done, click “Update Reminder”

    Note: In this example, the event will happen on 3:00 PM, so change the time to 3:00 PM.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the reminder send-time field and Update Reminder button. Set the reminder to the session start time before saving the change.

prose.body_md:
How to Update Project of the Week Reminders on Luma
This document shows the steps on how to Update Project of the Week Reminders on Luma to send a reminder everyday to the participants.

Project of the Week Description: [https://docs.google.com/document/d/18rCX0Fhkq2KK4pi_lTHMR9yRaIHY2-956zoOyBRsvRE/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/document/d/18rCX0Fhkq2KK4pi_lTHMR9yRaIHY2-956zoOyBRsvRE/edit?usp=sharing)

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Create events in Meetup.com
frontmatter.summary: Steps to copy the announcement from Luma to Meetup, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Events/Create events in Meetup.com.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Copy the announcement from Luma to Meetup.
- Outcome: We use meetup.com as an extra way to bring more people to our events.
- Trigger: After creating an event in Luma.
- Frequency: Per event.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club Meetup group.
- Tools: Meetup, YouTube.
- Inputs: Event title, date, time, duration, image, description, and stream link.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you will need to do is go to "[www.meetup.com/home](https://www.meetup.com/home/?suggested=true&source=EVENTS)” click "Create event".
    [https://www.meetup.com/home/?suggested=true&source=EVENTS](https://www.meetup.com/home/?suggested=true&source=EVENTS)![](../../../images/events/create-events-in-meetup-com/media/image3.jpg)
    Image note: The screenshot shows Meetup's home area where the Create event action starts a new DataTalks.Club listing. Use it after logging into the correct Meetup account.

After, you can now fill in the necessary details or copy the details of the event you created on “[luma.com](https://lu.ma/calendar/manage/cal-kg9jR2UeDILH68H)”

    Note: In this example, our event name is "Using Data to Create Liveable Cities”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Meetup event form alongside details copied from Lu.ma. It establishes Lu.ma as the source for the title, date, description, and image.

flat_steps.body_md: Paste the event name under the "Title (required)" on [Meetup.com](https://www.meetup.com/home/?suggested=true&source=EVENTS)

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Title (required) field in the Meetup editor. Paste the Lu.ma event name here so the Meetup listing matches the original event.

flat_steps.body_md:
After pasting the title, choose your date and time.

    Note: For this example, we are having Mon, September 23, 2024, as our date and 12:30 CEST as the preferred time for the event.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Meetup date and time selector with an example date and 12:30 CEST time. Use it to match the event schedule from Lu.ma.

flat_steps.body_md:
And click the drag-down button for the duration of the event.

    Note: In this example, the time duration of the event is 1 hour.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the duration dropdown in the Meetup time section. Select the expected event length, such as one hour for the example event.

flat_steps.body_md: To add a photo, select “upload photo”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Upload photo control in the Meetup event image area. Use it to add the same banner used on the Lu.ma event.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, select your photo on your computer and click "open"

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the local file picker for choosing the event image. Select the correct downloaded banner and open it for upload.

flat_steps.body_md: Crop your photo if needed and click "Save photo"

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Meetup crop dialog for the uploaded event photo. Adjust the crop if needed, then save it so the banner appears on the event page.

flat_steps.body_md: Once done, copy the description of the event from “Lu.ma”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Lu.ma event description used as the source text for Meetup. Copy the full description before switching back to the Meetup editor.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, paste it under "Description" on Meetup.com

    Note: You can edit necessary adjustments with the description you pasted like

    remove spacing, add bullets, etc.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Meetup Description field after pasting the Lu.ma text. Clean up spacing and bullets here so the listing is readable.

flat_steps.body_md:
Don't forget to add the link to the slack community.
    “Join our Slack: https://datatalks.club/slack.html”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Slack community line added to the event description. Include the `https://datatalks.club/slack.html` link so attendees can join the community.

flat_steps.body_md:
For the location, click the “Online" and add the link
    of DataTalk's youtube channel: [https://www.youtube.com/c/DataTalksClub](https://www.youtube.com/c/DataTalksClub).

    Note: Double-check that the location and YouTube link is correct.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Online location field with the DataTalks.Club YouTube channel URL. This is the standard location for Meetup events streamed on YouTube.

flat_steps.body_md: The next thing to do is un toggle the check button for the "Registration form"

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Registration form setting in the Meetup editor. Turn it off so attendees are not asked unnecessary custom questions.

flat_steps.body_md: After reviewing your work, click "Publish"

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Publish button after the Meetup event details are complete. Use it only after checking the title, schedule, image, description, location, and registration setting.

flat_steps.body_md: After clicking, select "Announce it now"

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows Meetup's announcement prompt after publishing. Select Announce it now to notify the group immediately.

frontmatter.title: Republishing meetup events from other communities to our meetup group
frontmatter.summary: Steps to cross-post partner community events to the DataTalks.Club Meetup group, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Events/Republishing meetup events from other communities to our meetup group.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Cross-post partner community events to the DataTalks.Club Meetup group.
- Outcome: The partner event is republished on Meetup with matching details.
- Trigger: Another community asks DataTalks.Club to promote an event.
- Frequency: As requested by partner communities.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club Meetup group.
- Tools: Meetup.
- Inputs: Partner event title, description, date, time, location, image, and host details.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is enter the title of the event under the “Title (required)” field.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Meetup event editor focused on the Title (required) field. Use it to copy the partner event title into the new Meetup listing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click the “Date and Time” field to indicate the date of the event.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Date and Time section of the Meetup form. This is where the partner event's scheduled date is selected.

flat_steps.body_md: Then change the time and the duration of the event.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the time and duration controls after the date picker is open. Match these values to the partner event so the reposted listing starts and ends correctly.

flat_steps.body_md: Next, to upload a photo, click “Upload Photo”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Meetup image area with the Upload Photo action. Use it to attach the banner or event image from the partner community.

flat_steps.body_md: Then double-click the photo of the event you downloaded on your computer.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the file picker used to select the downloaded event image. Choose the correct local banner file before returning to Meetup.

flat_steps.body_md:
To proceed, copy and paste the description of the event under the “Description” field

    Note: Make sure to follow proper spacing and line breaks when editing the description of the event.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Description field in the Meetup editor. Paste the partner event description here and keep the formatting readable.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, copy the location of the event and paste it under the “Add venue” field and click the suggest venue powered by Google.

    Note: Always double-check the location of the event if it’s correct or not.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Add venue field and Google's suggested venue result. Select the matching venue so the location on the republished event is accurate.

flat_steps.body_md:
Then untoggle the button under the “Venue Type” and select the “Indoor” option for the venue type.

    Note: Since the event doesn’t require any mask or COVID-19 vaccination card, leave the options empty. If ever it is required, untoggle the different buttons.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Venue Type options with Indoor selected. It also shows where to leave health requirement toggles off when they do not apply.

flat_steps.body_md: Now, to add the host of the event, enter his/her name under “Hosts” and click the name suggested.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Hosts field with a suggested person result. Choose the correct host from the suggestion list instead of leaving the text as an unmatched entry.

flat_steps.body_md: After you review the details of the event, click “Publish”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the final Meetup review screen with the Publish button. Use it after checking the title, timing, venue, image, host, and description.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, select “Announce it now”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the post-publish announcement prompt. Select Announce it now so Meetup immediately notifies the group about the republished event.

prose.body_md:
How to Republish Meetup Events from other communities to our Meetup Group.
This procedure will show you the steps on how to Republish Meetup Events from other communities to our Meetup Group.

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Rescheduling Already Passed Event in Meetup
frontmatter.summary: Steps to recreate a past Meetup event that cannot be edited and notify attendees about the new event.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Events/Rescheduling Already Passed Event in Meetup.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: If an event was scheduled but did not happen, and the date has already passed, Meetup does not allow editing past events. A new event must be created, and attendees from the previous event must be notified.
- Outcome:
- Trigger:
- Frequency:

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access:
- Tools:
- Inputs:

flat_steps.body_md: Log in to Meetup.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Meetup account area after login. Start from the correct organizer account before looking for the past event.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on "Past" to view events that have already ended.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Past tab in the Meetup event list. Use it because Meetup does not let you reschedule an event that has already ended.

flat_steps.body_md: Scroll down and select on the event that passed that needs to be rescheduled.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the list of past Meetup events. Open the specific event that needs to be duplicated for the new date.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on "Duplicate" to create a copy of the event.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Duplicate action on the past event. Duplicating preserves the topic, speaker, and description so only the schedule needs updating.

flat_steps.body_md: Use the same event details (topic, speaker, description), but update the date and time for the new schedule. Proceed with creating the event.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the duplicated Meetup event editor with the date and time fields available. Keep the event content the same, then set the new schedule before publishing.

flat_steps.body_md: Go back to the past event and click on "Organizer Tools" and Select "Contact attendees" from the dropdown options.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows Organizer Tools on the original past event with Contact attendees available. Use that option to notify people who registered for the cancelled date.

flat_steps.body_md:
Scroll down to Subject and Message then type in the template below.

    Note: Copy the link from the new Meetup event we just created.

    Subject: “New date for the Interview”

    Message: “Hi there,

frontmatter.title: Check if a person already exists on our website and find their ID
frontmatter.summary: Steps to check whether a guest already has a website profile and find their existing speaker ID.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Events/Check if a person already exists on our website and find their ID.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: We want to check if a guest already has a profile on our website
- Outcome: If they do, we don’t need to add them again
- Trigger: before adding them on the website and filling in the Airtable form ([Create speaker profiles in Airtable](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1PaX3fYo7grHvQ2d7Mw1LBXZidJmFXqJ6ttk-DUeLNXM/edit))
- Frequency: Per speaker or guest.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club website and website repository.
- Tools: Website people page, GitHub search.
- Inputs: Person name and, when needed, email.

flat_steps.body_md: Go to [https://datatalks.club/people.html](https://datatalks.club/people.html)

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the DataTalks.Club people page where existing speaker profiles are listed. This is the page to search before creating a new profile.

flat_steps.body_md:
Press “Ctrl + F” to open the search box and type the name or last name of the speaker. You will see that relevant entries are highlighted as you type


    If the person exists, you will find their name there. Otherwise, you won’t.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the browser search highlighting matching names on the people page. It helps confirm whether the speaker already has a profile entry.

flat_steps.body_md:
If the person exists, click on their profile and note their speaker ID – it will be in the URL:


    For “Alex Kim”, the profile URL is [https://datatalks.club/people/alexkim.html](https://datatalks.club/people/alexkim.html), and the ID is “alexkim”. In 99% of cases, the ID is first name + last name, all lowercase, no spaces.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the profile page URL after opening an existing person record. The speaker ID is the lowercase slug at the end of that URL.

frontmatter.title: Create speaker profiles via Airtable form
frontmatter.summary: Steps for adding a new speaker to the website that we previously didn't take part in our events, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Events/Create speaker profiles via Airtable form.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Adding a new speaker to the website that we previously didn’t take part in our events
- Outcome: We want people to know who the speaker is, so we want to create a profile for them on our website
- Trigger: After we announced the event on luma but before we fill in the event form (see [Fill in the “event” form in Airtable for adding events to our website](https://docs.google.com/document/u/1/d/1DEpKCmIGwoOE-erFoUrH6hSO2TB9wcDgZF_S1I395Q8/edit))!
- Frequency: Per new person.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Speaker profile Airtable form.
- Tools: Airtable, LinkedIn, Google search.
- Inputs: Email, name, bio, photo, and profile links.

TODO:

- Examples for profiles

groups.title: Checking if we already have the information about the guest

steps.body_md:
Follow [Check if a person already exists on our website and find their ID](check-if-a-person-already-exists-on-our-website-and-find-their-id.md) to see if the person is already on our website.

    If they are, you don’t add them to the form and don’t follow the rest of the process.
    Instead, [Add email for people who are already on the website](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1SZiocwZ_2-TwUn7uKBLtdVIpy6bWp5KQQy6wodVWaa0/edit). This step is not necessary if we already have their email in our database – so skip it if you remember that we already processed this person recently.
Open the form: [https://airtable.com/app7NCWvFj6Wz0ASm/shrKQwXkdEOLLX8H7](https://airtable.com/app7NCWvFj6Wz0ASm/shrKQwXkdEOLLX8H7).
The first thing you need to do is add the speaker's email.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the speaker profile Airtable form at the email field. Enter the speaker's contact email here so the profile can be connected to future event records.

steps.body_md: After which, input the name of the Speaker.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the name field in the Airtable speaker form. Use the speaker's public name exactly as it should appear on the website.

steps.body_md:
Next is to paste the Biography of the speaker.

    Note: The biography of the speaker can usually be copied in the publisher's website under "About the author"

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the biography field where the speaker's short bio is pasted. This text becomes the profile description, so it should be cleaned up before submission.

steps.body_md:
Then paste the link of the speaker's picture URL.

    The picture URL of the speaker can be found in their LinkedIn or Twitter account by copying the image address.

    If we don’t have a link to their image, or they provided the picture themselves over email, you can attach the file by clicking "Attach File"

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the photo attachment area with the Attach File option. Use it when the speaker provided an image file instead of a public image URL.

steps.body_md:
Add the github profile if available

    Note: Paste only the part after "https://github.com/".

    Example: “https://github.com/wrussell1999” and we will only put “wrussell1999”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the GitHub field filled with only the username value. It demonstrates that the form expects `wrussell1999`, not the full GitHub URL.

steps.body_md:
Next is to add the LinkedIn profile of the speaker.

    Note: Only include the part after "https://www.linkedin.com/in/"

    Example: “https://www.linkedin.com/in/wrussell1999/” and we will only put “wrussell1999” without any slash at the end.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the LinkedIn field using just the profile slug. Remove the `linkedin.com/in/` prefix and any trailing slash before entering it.

steps.body_md:
And then, type the Twitter account of the author if available.

    Note: Only the username of the author must be included.

    Example: “https://x.com/wrussell1999” and we will only put “wrussell1999”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Twitter/X field with only the handle text. Enter the username without the full `x.com` URL.

steps.body_md:
Don't also forget to paste the full website of the speaker

    Note: If the author has no website, just leave it blank.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the website field, which is the one profile link that should remain a full URL. Leave it blank if the speaker does not have a personal site.

steps.body_md: And lastly, after reviewing the form, click "Submit”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Submit button at the end of the Airtable form. Use it only after the email, name, bio, photo, and social links have been reviewed.

steps.body_md:
After submitting the form, trigger the Github actions by following this process document:

    [Update the website with the information from forms](../../../media/podcast/sops/update-the-website-with-the-information-from-forms.md)

frontmatter.title: Fill in the “event” form in Airtable for adding events to our website
frontmatter.summary: Steps to fill out the event form for speaker details and event information, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Events/Fill in the “event” form in Airtable for adding events to our website.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Fill out the event form for speaker details and event information.
- Outcome: Later the information from this form will be used for publishing the event on our website.
- Trigger: After announcing the event on luma.
- Frequency: Per event.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Airtable event form.
- Tools: Airtable, Luma.
- Inputs: Speaker email, title, Luma URL, event type, and date.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you will be doing is open the [events form](https://airtable.com/app7NCWvFj6Wz0ASm/shrAY1W58LlKGGq81). Then, enter the speaker's email and the event
    title.

    Note: The event title should be the same as in Luma.


    Note: If there are 2 guests, follow this [Adding People to Multiple Events](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-TtQLALFoMM1lebB1b2qurJYhL7M5Bsn2QUnHfk8ZOY/edit?tab=t.0) after sending this form. Since we can only add one email.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Airtable event form fields for speaker email and event title. Use the same title as Lu.ma so the website entry matches the public event.

flat_steps.body_md: To enter the Event title, visit the event on Luma and click on “Copy Link” at the top.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Lu.ma event page with the Copy Link control. Copy this public event URL for the Airtable form.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, go back to the form and paste the URL under "URL"

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the URL field in the Airtable event form. Paste the copied Lu.ma link there so the website can point to the registration page.

flat_steps.body_md: To proceed, select the event type, click on the drop down button and choose what kind of event.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the event type dropdown in the Airtable form. Choose the type that matches the event format, such as podcast, webinar, or workshop.

flat_steps.body_md: And lastly, pick the date of the event and select "Submit"

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the date field and Submit button at the end of the event form. Submit only after the date, type, URL, speaker email, and title are filled in.

frontmatter.title: Get the analytics of an event
frontmatter.summary: Steps to get accurate attendance analytics for a YouTube live event, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Events/Get the analytics of an event.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Get accurate attendance analytics for a YouTube live event.
- Outcome: A screenshot or number from YouTube Analytics shows how many people actually attended.
- Trigger: Someone asks how many people attended a webinar or live event.
- Frequency: As needed after events.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: YouTube Studio access for the event video.
- Tools: YouTube Analytics.
- Inputs: YouTube event video link.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is click “Edit video”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the YouTube video page with the Edit video control used to open the event in YouTube Studio. It confirms you are starting from the correct event video before looking for analytics.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, select “Analytics”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Analytics tab inside YouTube Studio for the selected event video. Use it to confirm you are viewing metrics for the right video before scrolling.

flat_steps.body_md:
To proceed, scroll down and you can locate the Concurrent viewers of the video.


    Note: If the live session has already ended, just scroll down in the “Analytics” tab to see the Number of viewers of the live broadcast.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows where the Concurrent viewers metric appears in the live analytics panel. This is the number to capture when reporting live attendance.
The screenshot shows the post-live analytics view after the event has ended. Use the viewer count shown there when concurrent live data is no longer available.

prose.body_md:

This procedure will show you the steps on how to get the analytics of an event.

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Update event dates on the DataTalks.Club website.
- Outcome: The website event data has the corrected date after the repository change is committed.
- Trigger: An event is rescheduled and the website date needs to change.
- Frequency: Whenever an event date changes.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club website GitHub repository.
- Tools: GitHub web editor.
- Inputs: Event entry in `_data/events.yaml` and the new event date.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open the [DataTalk’s Github repo](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/datatalksclub.github.io/blob/main/_data/events.yaml).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Update dates of the events on the website process by showing the screen for open the DataTalk's Github repo. Look for the red box or arrow around Open, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on the pen tool icon to edit the date.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the Update dates of the events on the website process by showing the screen for click on the pen tool icon to edit the date. Look for the red box or arrow around Edit, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Announce event in Slack in #announcements
frontmatter.summary: Steps to announce upcoming events in \\#announcements channel to inform the community, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Events/Announce event in Slack in #announcements.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Announce upcoming events in \#announcements channel to inform the community.
- Outcome: So more people can see and join the event.
- Trigger: Post right after making the calendar event (Luma, Meet Up and Google Calendar) or as soon as possible
- Frequency:

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access:
- Tools:
- Inputs:

flat_steps.body_md: Open the DataTalksClub Slack, and click the “#announcement” channel.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the DataTalks.Club Slack workspace with the #announcement channel selected. Post event announcements there so the community sees them in the right channel.

flat_steps.body_md: Once \#announcement channel is open, go to the [slack announcement template](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1sGv9tZY2DB1AqBMVWklZS-B-mzRTaB7VllAJBNmeoxo/edit?usp=sharing) and copy the language and emojis or you can copy the latest announcement on the \#announcement channel.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the announcement template source used for Slack posts. Copy its structure and emojis before editing the message for the upcoming event.

flat_steps.body_md:
Paste the copied language for announcement to the message box then edit the text based on the upcoming event.

    Join us for the "Title of the event" Event!


    Speaker:


    Date:


    Time: 24:00 (CET) (*Always use 24 hour time format, example 1:30 PM it should be 13:30 CET* )


    This event focuses on XX (*You can use the title or generate the description of the event using Chat GPT as a one line description.*)

    Register here: (Luma Link)


    Note: For this example we will be using the podcast event “Human-Centered AI for Disordered Speech Recognition with Katarzyna Foremniak.”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the first line of the Slack announcement with the event title placeholder. Replace it with the exact title of the upcoming event.
The screenshot shows the speaker line in the Slack announcement template. Fill it with the event speaker's name so readers can quickly identify who is presenting.
The screenshot shows the date line in the Slack announcement. Update it with the scheduled event date from Lu.ma or the calendar.
The screenshot shows the time line with the required 24-hour CET format. Convert any AM/PM source time before posting the announcement.
The screenshot shows the registration line where the Lu.ma link belongs. Paste the event's actual Lu.ma URL so Slack readers can register.
The screenshot shows the completed announcement text for the example podcast event. Use it as a model for the one-line event focus sentence.

flat_steps.body_md: Add the banner photo for the event (usually the rectangular format). Click the (+) plus sign on the lower left corner and select “Upload from your computer”. Then, select the photo.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows Slack's plus menu for uploading the event banner from your computer. Attach the rectangular event image before sending the announcement.

flat_steps.body_md: After pasting and editing the text of the announcement you can hit enter or click the green arrow button.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the composed Slack message with the send control available. Send it only after the text, Lu.ma link, and attached banner are correct.

flat_steps.body_md: Click the link to double check that it is the right Luma Link for the event.

Make sure the file name is relevant to the event. To edit, click the three dots on the upper right of the image you uploaded and select “Edit file details”.

    Note: You can also rename it in the File Manager before attaching the image in Slack.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the file menu on the uploaded Slack image with Edit file details. Use it to rename the attachment when the file name does not clearly match the event.

flat_steps.body_md: Change the file name to anything relevant to the event. Then, click “Save changes.”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows Slack's file details dialog with the name field and Save changes button. Enter an event-specific file name so the uploaded banner is easy to identify later.

frontmatter.title: Adding paid invoices to the bookkeeping spreadsheet and Dropbox
frontmatter.summary: Record paid sponsor invoices in the bookkeeping spreadsheet and store the invoice PDF in Dropbox.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Bookkeeping & Invoices/Adding paid invoices to the bookkeeping spreadsheet and adding it to dropbox_.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Record paid sponsor invoices in the bookkeeping spreadsheet and store the invoice PDF in Dropbox.
- Outcome: The paid invoice is available in Dropbox and reflected in the bookkeeping spreadsheet for the correct payment month.
- Trigger: A sponsor invoice has been paid.
- Frequency: As needed

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Dropbox income invoices folder, bookkeeping spreadsheet, and Finom.
- Tools: Finom, Dropbox, Google Sheets.
- Inputs: Paid invoice PDF, issue date, payment date, sponsor/company name, transaction description, and paid amount.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you need to do is download the PDF file of the invoice.

   Note: You can also download the PDF file on Finom

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the red callout around the highlighted amount, recipient, transaction row, or proof-of-payment control, then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Upload the downloaded PDF to the [income-invoices Dropbox folder](https://www.dropbox.com/home/_dtc_paperwork/income-invoices).

   Rename the file using this format:

   ```text
   +YYYY-MM-DD-<NAME OF SPONSOR>
   ```

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the red callout around the highlighted amount, recipient, transaction row, or proof-of-payment control, then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Open the [bookkeeping spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/u/0/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit).
Enter the invoice issue date in the `Date` column.

Enter the payment date in the `Date Paid` column.

   Dates should use this format:

   ```text
   YYYY-MM-DD
   ```

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the red callout around the highlighted amount, recipient, transaction row, or proof-of-payment control, then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Enter the company that paid the invoice in the `Provider` column.
Enter the transaction description in the `What` column.

Enter the paid amount.

   Amounts should use this format:

   ```text
   0000.00
   ```


   Important: sponsor invoices are included in reports only when they are marked as paid. Record the invoice in the month when payment was received, not the month when the invoice was created.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the red callout around the highlighted amount, recipient, transaction row, or proof-of-payment control, then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

validation.body_md:
## Validation


- The invoice PDF is in the Dropbox income invoices folder.
- The Dropbox filename follows `+YYYY-MM-DD-<NAME OF SPONSOR>`.
- The bookkeeping spreadsheet contains the issue date, payment date, provider, description, and paid amount.
- The entry is recorded for the month when payment was received.

troubleshooting.body_md:
## Troubleshooting


- If the invoice is missing from reports, check whether it is marked as paid.
- If the dates look wrong in the spreadsheet, confirm they use `YYYY-MM-DD`.

frontmatter.title: Creating Invoices for Stripe Technology Europe Ltd Payout in Finom
frontmatter.summary: Create manual Finom invoices for Stripe Technology Europe payouts so income is recorded for bookkeeping and tax reporting.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Bookkeeping & Invoices/Creating Invoices for Stripe Technology Europe Ltd Payout in Finom.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose:
- Outcome:
- Trigger:
- Frequency:

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access:
- Tools:
- Inputs:

flat_steps.body_md: Log in to [Finom](https://app.finom.co/en/signin) account. Click “New Invoice” in the main dashboard.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "New Invoice", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: In the "Send to:" field, click the dropdown menu and select "Stripe Technology Europe Ltd" from the options.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Stripe Technology Europe Ltd", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
In the "Items" field, type “AI Shipping Labs Membership Fees (Date)”.

    Note: Ask Alexey for the specific revenue period to include in this description.

    In the “Unit” field, select “period”.
    In the "Price" field, enter the exact payout amount.
    Note: You can find this amount in the downloaded statement from either Finom or Revolut.

    Under the "VAT" column, click the dropdown and select "Exempt from Tax".

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Exempt from Tax", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.
This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Exempt from Tax", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Since the country is in Ireland and they are part of EU countries it's 0% tax (with note "VAT 0%: reverse charge”)

    Click on “ADD PAYMENT TERMS” at the bottom left of the invoice.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in Finom. Look for the red callout around "ADD PAYMENT TERMS", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Type in “VAT 0%: reverse charge” and click “Save”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Save", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
In the "Issue date" field, enter the date the transaction actually occurred.
    Set the "Due date" to Net 30 (exactly one month after the issue date).

    Review all entered information for accuracy, then click the "Save" button.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Save", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on Download.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
Open your File Manager and locate the downloaded invoice.

    Rename the file using the format: YYYY-MM-DD - \[Recipient Name\].
    In this example,for a payout to Stripe Technology Europe Ltd on March 03, the file should be named 2026-03-05-Stripe Technology Europe Ltd.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

frontmatter.title: Creating invoices for Valeriia
frontmatter.summary: Create Valeriia monthly invoices by updating her Google Docs invoice template for the correct period.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Bookkeeping & Invoices/Creating invoices for Valeriia.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Use Valeriia’s invoice template to create her monthly invoice.
- Outcome: Valeriia’s invoice is updated from the template for the correct month.
- Trigger: Towards the end of the month that will be invoiced or beginning of the month after.
- Frequency: Monthly

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Valeriia invoice template in Google Docs.
- Tools: Google Docs.
- Inputs: Invoice month, invoice number, work period, and invoice amount.

steps.body_md:
Open the template through [this link](https://docs.google.com/document/d/10Z9Iiev6Uj2Dq0_BiiwvW2PppWPuNFfJwCI-M2Mcs9M/edit).

    Note: Throughout this document, we will update the template below, which is Valeriia’s August 2024 invoice.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in the workflow. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

steps.body_md:
There will be a few changes each month. First, change the invoice number to the reflect the current month in this format: “YYYYM-1” or “YYYYMM-1” for double-digit months.

    Note: In this example the invoice number is 20249-1 because the invoice is for Valeriia’s work in September 2024*.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in the workflow. Look for the red callout around "YYYYMM-1", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

steps.body_md: Change the “Invoice date” to the present day (the day you’re creating the invoice). Follow the format, “DD.MM.YYYY”, as shown below.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in the workflow. Look for the red callout around "DD.MM.YYYY", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

steps.body_md: Update the months after “Services provided in” with the previous month and the current month.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in the workflow. Look for the red callout around "Services provided in", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

steps.body_md: Update the items in the table. Valeriia’s usual monthly tasks are “Email newsletter creation” and “Social media management”. Ask Valeriia on Telegram if she has additional items to add for that month.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in the workflow. Look for the red callout around "Social media management", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

steps.body_md: To add new items, hover over the lowest row and click the plus sign (+) that appears on the left. This will add a new row. Type the description of the task in each new row under “Item” and the price under “Price, EUR”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in the workflow. Look for the red callout around "Price, EUR", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

steps.body_md: Finally, calculate the total and fill in the “Price total” and “Invoice total”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in the workflow. Look for the red callout around "Invoice total", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

frontmatter.title: Creating Invoices in Finom
frontmatter.summary: Create and send sponsor invoices in Finom, including company details, VAT handling, invoice items, payment links, and bookkeeping follow-up.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Bookkeeping & Invoices/Creating Invoices in Finom.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Create sponsor invoices in Finom.
- Outcome: A Finom invoice is created with the correct sponsor, items, tax, and bank details.
- Trigger: A sponsor or client needs an invoice.
- Frequency: As needed

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Finom and sponsor billing details.
- Tools: Finom.
- Inputs: Sponsor name, billing address, VAT ID if applicable, invoice items, amount, dates, and bank details.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open Finom and click “Documents”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Documents", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on “New Invoice”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "New Invoice", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
Here are the bank details for [Revolut](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1LbXLTEjS_APb5X9HAyfD1a_aL3LPIVzzXpnGb9amqac/edit?tab=t.0) and [Finom](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1beDJyHfV5e68swhRi1OGD2f6wsw6H6K7L7p3_i5G2Lk/edit?tab=t.0). Ensure that the IBAN on the invoice matches one of these two accounts. Currently we are using the Revolut IBAN unless advised otherwise by Alexey.

    Note: After updating the IBAN on an invoice, all subsequent invoices will automatically use the most recently updated IBAN. Always double-check the IBAN at the end of creating each invoice to ensure it is correct.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Select the dropdown arrow and select the sponsor. For a new sponsor, we click “New Contact”. If they are an old sponsor, you should be able to select them from the drop-down list.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "New Contact", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
Enter the Country and Business name of the sponsor, VAT ID number and the Address. Then click on “Save”.
    In the Email, type in your email address.
    We have to add VAT ID Number for counties within the EU. For other countries, it's an option.

    If the Invoice was already been made and needs updating to add the VAT ID number follow this process: [Updating Invoices with VAT-ID Requirements](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OUA41n5PVjTun4vrNmM9O377uIVrDTZ6Xf2XnKuS1S0/edit?tab=t.0)

    Note: Finom now requires an email address when adding contacts. You can use your own email address to proceed. This email will not appear on the invoice itself.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted amount, recipient, transaction row, or proof-of-payment control, then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
In the “Items” section, click the empty field. Add the items.
    Make sure to use the use the European date format for the invoice (DD MMM YYYY)

    Note: In creating an invoice, it’s enough to have one invoice for multiple dates as long as they have the same content and description (e.g. social media promotion, newsletter promotion and etc).

    Example: Newsletter Sponsorship 05 May 2025

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
If the client requests to add a note on the invoice(proceed with steps 8-9, if there are no notes to be added skip and continue to step 10).

    In this example Alexey mentioned that client want to add “SERVICES AGREEMENT No. 1784”


    Click on “ADD CUSTOMER NOTES”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "SERVICES AGREEMENT No. 1784", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.
This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "ADD CUSTOMER NOTES", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Type in the notes and click “Save”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Save", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
Click on the dropdown under “Unit” and select what applies to the Invoice if it’s:

    - Workshop - workshop

    - Newsletter - email = €1,500 for Primary, €1,000 for secondary and €2,500 for stand-alone

    - Social Media posts - post = €1,200

    - Course - course

    Note: Please check with Alexey with the updated amount

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, enter the amount under “Price (net)”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Price (net)", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next, select the right VAT.

    The rules are as follows:

    - For Germany, it's 19% (follow steps 12-A).
    - For other EU countries, it's 0% with the note "VAT 0%: reverse charge" (follow steps on 12-B).
    - For other countries, it's 0% and the note is not needed (follow steps 12-C).

    12-A. If the client is in Germany:

    Add a 19 VAT (%). No need to for “ADD PAYMENT TERMS”.

    For example, the course price is € 2,000 x19% VAT = € 380

    Total Payment with Vat is € 2,380

    € 2,000 + € 380 VAT = € 2,380


    12-B. If the client is not in Germany but in the EU countries:

    Click on “ADD PAYMENT TERMS” at the bottom left of the invoice.

    Note: This note is only present for EU countries, but not for Germany or non-EU countries.


    Type in “VAT 0%: reverse charge” and click “Save”.


    12-C. If the client is not in Germany or EU: 0% VAT and the note is not needed.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted amount, recipient, transaction row, or proof-of-payment control, then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.
This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in Finom. Look for the red callout around "ADD PAYMENT TERMS", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.
This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Save", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Don’t forget to change and edit the Invoice No.
    Select the current date as the Invoice number.

    Note: For invoice numbers, it should follow this format YYYYMM/Number. In this example, the month is March 18. Make sure that the month on the invoice number is the same with the month you issued the invoice.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
Click the field on the Due Date, and select the date after 30 days.

    Note: For the Astronomer sponsor, they are Net 60, so the due date is after 60 days.

    Example: Today is March 18, 2025, the 30th day after is on April 17, 2025

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Once done, click “Save”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Save", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
To view and download the invoice, click “Preview PDF”


    The file name should follow this format: YYYY-MM-\<SPONSOR_NAME\>-datatalksclub-\<WHAT\>, where \<WHAT\> is “workshop”, “newsletter” or “sponsorship”.

    How to Create Invoice for Newsletter Promotion

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Preview PDF", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
In the “New Invoice” tab, select the Sponsor and on the “Item”, click “Promotion in newsletter (19.09.2022)
    Note: Change the date of the item. Follow the format DD.MM.YYYY. You can view the date of the promotion in the [Schedule Spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit#gid=1710801712)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Item", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Enter the amount under “Price (net)” and since it’s in Germany, the VAT (%) is 19.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Price (net)", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
If the sponsor has more than 1 newsletter promotion, click “ADD ITEM”

    Note: Don’t forget to change and edit the date of the newsletter promotion.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "ADD ITEM", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Once done, click “Save”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Save", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: To add a discount, click the three dots beside the amount and then add the discount under “Discount”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Discount", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Once the invoice is saved, click the three dots beside “Preview PDF” and click “Download as PDF” from the drop-down list.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around "Download as PDF", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: Rename the PDF. The file name should follow this format: YYYY-MM-\<SPONSOR_NAME\>-datatalksclub-\<WHAT\>, where \<WHAT\> is “workshop”, “newsletter” or “sponsorship”

Send the PDF of the invoice you created in response to the email thread with the sponsor.

    Note: [Link to template for emailing](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1F-NRfov0-X0lZ7jDwlUpko932liRSe_LhzRaX9G2UvI/edit?tab=t.0)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

frontmatter.title: Crosschecking with Revolut and Finom
frontmatter.summary: Crosscheck Revolut and Finom invoices against the bookkeeping spreadsheet to avoid missing transactions in tax reporting.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Bookkeeping & Invoices/Crosschecking with Revolut and Finom_.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Crosscheck all invoices, to ensure they’re recorded in the bookkeeping sheet
- Outcome: To ensure accurate tax reporting and avoid missing any transactions.
- Trigger: First week of every month.
- Frequency:

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access:
- Tools:
- Inputs:

steps.body_md: Open the [bookkeeping](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit?gid=784732145#gid=784732145) sheet and review the tax report for the month.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the bookkeeping spreadsheet view used for cross-checking. Look for the highlighted date and transaction rows, then compare them against the matching Revolut and Finom records.

steps.body_md: Log in to [Finom](https://app.finom.co/en/money) and get the Finom Statement [Creating Bank Statements in Finom](creating-bank-statements-in-finom.md)
Log in to [Revolut](https://business.revolut.com/overview) and get the Revolut Statement [Creating Bank Statements in Revolut](creating-bank-statements-in-revolut.md)
Open [Dropbox](https://www.dropbox.com/home/_dtc_paperwork/invoices), this is where all invoices and statements are stored.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in Finom. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

steps.body_md:
For each transaction in the [bookkeeping sheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit?gid=819898795#gid=819898795https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit?gid=819898795#gid=819898795), ensure these columns are correct
Column Description / Action
Date Enter the date in this format: YYYY-MM-DD
Provider Enter the name of the company that paid the amount.
What Enter a clear description of the transaction.
Price in USD If the invoice is issued in USD, record the original amount in USD here.
Price in EUR

Record the amount in EUR (this is the main currency column).

FOR AMOUNT IN USD: After recording the USD amount:

• If EUR conversion is shown in the statement, record that EUR amount.

• If not shown, use Wise for conversion.
Statement

Indicate whether the transaction appears in Finom or Revolut based on the downloaded statements.

• If not found in either, leave this cell blank.

• Make a separate list of all missing transactions (those not in Finom or Revolut) so they can be requested from Alexey later.
Count

Internal check: Count all invoices for the month and ensure the total matches the invoices in Dropbox.

• Note any missing invoices for Alexey.

Cross-check the following:
    - Bookkeeping Sheet vs. Finom Statement

    - Bookkeeping Sheet vs. Revolut Statement

    - Bookkeeping Sheet vs. Invoices in Dropbox.

Take note of discrepancies:
    - Transactions missing from both statements

    - Transactions in the statements but missing in the bookkeeping sheet

    - Any mismatched amounts between invoices and statements

    Note: If an invoice was paid in advance, include it in the month it was paid.

    Compare the invoices listed in both platforms with those in the bookkeeping sheet.

    Ensure that all paid invoices are captured, and no transaction has been overlooked.

Make a list of all missing Invoices/Receipts and Statements then make a request to Alexey by sending it via Telegram

    Note: Make sure that the date is included in each transaction to make it easier to find the documents.

    Format: \* Date - Provider

    Example: Invoice of [z.ai](http://z.ai) is missing in the dropbox, and Google One is not found in either Finom or Revolut, list it separately

    “Hi Alexey,

    For August 2025 Tax report we need the following:

    Invoices
    \* 2 Aug 2025 - Z.ai

    Statements
    \*11 Aug 2025 - Google One”
After receiving the documents file theme in the dropbox. And continue cross checking to make sure that there will be no missed transactions

frontmatter.title: Dealing with partially paid invoices (that are paid partially because of currency conversions)
frontmatter.summary: Handle invoices short-paid because of currency conversion differences and document the remaining balance.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Bookkeeping & Invoices/Dealing with partially paid invoices (that are paid partially because of currency conversions)_.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Handle invoices that are short-paid because of currency conversion differences.
- Outcome: The invoice record explains the partial payment and remaining difference.
- Trigger: A payment amount is lower than the invoice total due to currency conversion.
- Frequency: As needed

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Finom invoice and payment details.
- Tools: Finom.
- Inputs: Invoice total, received amount, currency conversion difference, and explanation text.

flat_steps.body_md: On the invoice, click the three-dotted button and select “Edit”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the workflow. Look for the red callout around "Edit", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click “Unlink payments and edit’

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the workflow. Look for the red callout around the highlighted amount, recipient, transaction row, or proof-of-payment control, then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
And on the price section, copy the amount that has been paid by the sponsor or company.

    Note: In this example, the company only paid \$14,682.25 instead of \$15,000 due to the currency conversion. For making USD invoice,* [*click here*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1cgm7SKXuDKP9Th6GXSHoJuUWNjctN9KoPS_yLVOHnGo/edit?usp=sharing)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the workflow. Look for the red callout around the highlighted amount, recipient, transaction row, or proof-of-payment control, then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click “Preview PDF”, and download the file.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the workflow. Look for the red callout around "Preview PDF", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, send the downloaded file to the sponsors and write them an email.

    Note: You can copy this template:

    ```text
    Hi!

    Due to the currency conversion, we received only this amount <ADD AMOUNT HERE>.

    We understand that it's a bit of hassle to add 500 more. So what we do instead is we create an invoice with the amount. And use this invoice for our tax report and if you need this, please find the attached PDF.
    ```

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the workflow. Look for the red callout around the highlighted amount, recipient, transaction row, or proof-of-payment control, then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

prose.body_md:
How to Deal with Partially Paid Invoices
This procedure will show you the steps on how to Deal with Partially Paid Invoices.

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: (For Update) Converting USD to EUR for Revolut transcations
frontmatter.summary: Convert non-EUR Revolut transactions, usually USD, into EUR values for the bookkeeping spreadsheet.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Bookkeeping & Invoices/(For Update) Converting USD to EUR for Revolut transcations.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Converting USD to EUR for our [bookkeeping](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/u/1/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit) spreadsheet
- Outcome: We make our tax report in EUR, not USD
- Trigger: When you see that there is a transaction with non-EUR currency (usually USD)
- Frequency: As needed

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Revolut transaction details and bookkeeping spreadsheet.
- Tools: Revolut, Google Sheets.
- Inputs: USD transaction amount, exchange rate, transaction date, and calculated EUR value.

flat_steps.body_md: In the Revolut page, see exchange rate value and copy (Ctrl +C).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot captures the currency-conversion evidence for the bookkeeping entry. Look for the highlighted exchange rate, fee, or calculated amount, then use that value when updating the EUR amount.

flat_steps.body_md: After copying, open a calculator. And compute Fees \* exchange rage
You can use Google Chrome for that: open another tab and on the search bar, paste (Ctrl +V) the copied value but erase “\$1 =”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot captures the currency-conversion evidence for the bookkeeping entry. Look for the highlighted exchange rate, fee, or calculated amount, then use that value when updating the EUR amount.

This screenshot captures the currency-conversion evidence for the bookkeeping entry. Look for the highlighted exchange rate, fee, or calculated amount, then use that value when updating the EUR amount.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, go back to the Revolut page and see the amount in USD and copy (Ctrl + C).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot captures the currency-conversion evidence for the bookkeeping entry. Look for the highlighted exchange rate, fee, or calculated amount, then use that value when updating the EUR amount.

flat_steps.body_md: After copying, paste (Ctrl +V) the copied value on the search bar and add the multiplication sign (\*) in between the two values then, press enter.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot captures the currency-conversion evidence for the bookkeeping entry. Look for the highlighted exchange rate, fee, or calculated amount, then use that value when updating the EUR amount.

flat_steps.body_md:
Upon pressing enter, results will be shown.

    Note: The obtained result will be the value used in the [bookkeeping spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit?usp=sharing).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot captures the currency-conversion evidence for the bookkeeping entry. Look for the highlighted exchange rate, fee, or calculated amount, then use that value when updating the EUR amount.

This screenshot captures the currency-conversion evidence for the bookkeeping entry. Look for the highlighted exchange rate, fee, or calculated amount, then use that value when updating the EUR amount.

prose.body_md:
How to convert USD to EUR for Revolut transactions

Use Revolut transaction evidence when it shows the settled EUR amount. If the EUR amount is not visible, use Wise historical rates for the transaction date and record the source/date in the DataOps task comment.

Step-by-step Instructions

validation.body_md:
## Validation

- The bookkeeping spreadsheet contains the EUR value, not only the original USD/non-EUR amount.
- The DataOps task comment records the conversion source and transaction date, or links to runtime conversion evidence when available.
- If Revolut shows the settled EUR charge, that amount is used before a generic calculator result.
- No unclear EUR amount remains before the monthly tax report is sent.

troubleshooting.body_md:
## Troubleshooting

- If Revolut does not show the settled EUR amount, use Wise historical rates for the transaction date and note the source in the task comment.
- If the transaction screenshot is missing, set the DataOps task to `waiting` with `waitingFor` set to the person or system that can provide it.
- If fees and exchange rate evidence disagree, ask Alexey or the accountant for the reporting rule before clearing the task.

frontmatter.title: (For updating) Adding expenses to the bookkeeping spreadsheet [Updating 10-02-2024]
frontmatter.summary: Add expense payments and receipts to the bookkeeping spreadsheet with supporting PDFs and correct fields.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Bookkeeping & Invoices/(For updating) Adding expenses to the bookkeeping spreadsheet [Updating 10-02-2024].docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Add expense payments and receipts to the bookkeeping spreadsheet.
- Outcome: The expense is recorded with the supporting PDF and correct bookkeeping fields.
- Trigger: Alexey forwards an expense email, receipt, or payment notification.
- Frequency: As needed

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Email, Dropbox, Finom, and bookkeeping spreadsheet.
- Tools: Gmail, Dropbox, Finom, Google Sheets.
- Inputs: Expense receipt or invoice, provider, payment date, amount, currency, and transaction details.

flat_steps.body_md:
Once Alexey forwarded the email notification and details of the payment, view the email and turn it into a PDF file. To do it, click the three-dotted button on the top left of the email and select “Print”

    Note: Sometimes, it could be a link to a PDF and needs to be downloaded.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the red callout around "Print", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click “Save”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot confirms the expense PDF is being saved from the browser. Look for the highlighted Save button, then save the document before attaching or recording the expense.

flat_steps.body_md: After saving it, create a new email and send it to Dropbox Invoice Attachment: dropboxinvoice.2ebx61@zapiermail.com.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the red callout around the highlighted customer, item, amount, date, tax, download, save, or send control, then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next, add the subject of the email. Include the PDF file you downloaded earlier by clicking the “clip” icon or dragging it in the email, and add the subject of the email.

    Note: In this example, the provider is “UberSuggest”

    Send only one invoice per email. Sending multiple invoices in a single email will automatically generate a zip file in the Dropbox Invoice folder, but we only need the PDF format, not the zip file.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the invoice detail or action needed in the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the red callout around "UberSuggest", then use it to verify the invoice before saving, downloading, or sending it.

flat_steps.body_md: After, open the [bookkeeping spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit?usp=sharing) and make sure that transaction details you sent earlier to the automation email is reflected on the [spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit?usp=sharing).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot confirms the automation email created a matching bookkeeping row. Look for the highlighted provider and amount in the spreadsheet, then compare them with the source invoice before editing fields.

flat_steps.body_md: Also, make sure to check the [dropbox](https://www.dropbox.com/home/_dtc_paperwork) folder and verify if the invoice we sent earlier is reflected.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot checks that the automated transaction details reached the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the highlighted provider, description, and amount cells, then verify they match the source transaction.

flat_steps.body_md: Edit the Provider’s name or the company you paid and the description of the transaction details or the transaction you made that we will pay for under the “What” column.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot verifies the payment evidence in the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the red callout around "What", then confirm the transaction matches the invoice or bookkeeping row before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: To view the description, open the PDF file and locate the description of the transaction.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the source PDF description that should drive the spreadsheet entry. Look for the highlighted service or subscription description, then copy that wording into the What column.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next, go back to the [bookkeeping spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit?usp=sharing) and change the description of the transaction under the “What” column and enter the amount.

    Note: Since it’s in Euro, leave the “Price, \$” column empty. Also, since we paid the amount expenses), it’s negative (-).

    For ILZ Consulting, there's an invoice for a recurring payment, so we don't need to attach the invoice with every declaration. When Alexey forwards a statement from Finom from ILZ Consulting, you just need to add it to the bookkeeping table; no need to add it on Dropbox.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot points to the PDF evidence used to fill the spreadsheet description. Look for the highlighted description line in the document, then copy that wording into the bookkeeping row.

flat_steps.body_md:
For transactions in USD (\$), open [Finom](https://app.finom.co/en/signin?redirect=%2Fen%2Fmoney) and select the transaction.

    Note: For transactions already in Euro, leave the “$” column empty.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot identifies the Finom transaction to use for USD expense conversion. Look for the highlighted transaction amount and merchant row, then copy the USD and EUR values for the spreadsheet.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, copy the amount in dollars and euros.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot shows the Finom transaction detail for a USD expense. Look for the highlighted transaction amount and source details, then use them to decide whether conversion is needed before updating the spreadsheet.

flat_steps.body_md:
And paste it on the [bookkeeping spreadsheet.](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jIBou5XvBY3uy7dsxDUVM4yiPZAgXUN5AZJN3bDJgHU/edit?usp=sharing)

    - If the payment was done via Finom, you can see the exact amount in EUR there.

    - If the payment was done outside of Finom, Alexey will give the exact value.

    - Sometimes, do the conversion with [Wise](https://wise.com/gb/currency-converter/usd-to-eur-rate/history/12-06-2024) because the payment was not made with a EUR card, so the exact bank conversion is unknown.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot confirms the copied USD and EUR values have been entered in the bookkeeping spreadsheet. Look for the highlighted amount cells, then verify both currency columns reconcile with Finom.

prose.body_md:
Adding expenses to the bookkeeping spreadsheet
This procedure will show you the steps on how to Fill up the Bookkeeping Spreadsheet and Send invoices. This procedure uses automation to do the job. It’s possible also to manually add the file to the dropbox.

Step-by-step Instructions
