frontmatter.title: Change the status to confirmed
frontmatter.summary: Steps to update an event status after a guest confirms the proposed date, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Change the status to _confirmed_.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Update an event status after a guest confirms the proposed date.
- Outcome: The schedule reflects the confirmed or ready status and follow-up tasks are clear.
- Trigger: The guest agrees to the proposed date.
- Frequency: Per confirmed event.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club schedule spreadsheet and calendar event.
- Tools: Google Sheets, Google Calendar.
- Inputs: Guest confirmation, proposed event row, podcast document link, and readiness state.

flat_steps.body_md:
The guest agrees to the previously proposed date email or LinkedIn.

    Note: For podcasts, if the guest is from CET or earlier and can do the podcast at noon, we should offer them noon, otherwise 17 pm

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next is to open the [DataTalks.Club schedule](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit#gid=0) and change "proposed date" to "confirmed"

    Note: If the status of the gesture newsletter promotion is still pending, add a “(?)” right before the name.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the correct record, field, or status before updating the workflow; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled (?). Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
For podcast event, change “confirmed” to ready if the questions/doc is ready to be announced.

    Note: Description of the states:
    Proposed - You’re proposing a date to the speaker

    Confirmed - The speaker agreed on the date

    Ready - The podcast document/questions are ready to be announced

    Announced - The event is announced across platforms such as Meetup, Luma, and LinkedIn.


    After this is done, you will need to

    - [Create a document with questions for the podcast](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-mrkUrADjMBYDrk2AS3-s604_Vc8prTPKlpWb4cd6U0/edit)

    - [Insert podcast document link to the podcast cell](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1N4nVUm9OR6WE_OYzSCKQwyLYD-my38sKgkGlWXiluWE/edit)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

prose.body_md:
How to change the status to “confirmed”
This procedure will show you the steps on how to change the status of “confirmed.”

Step-by-step Instructions

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Collect verified winner emails for a giveaway campaign.
- Outcome: Winner emails are recorded and can be sent to the publisher contact.
- Trigger: Giveaway winners respond with their email addresses.
- Frequency: Per giveaway campaign.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Social media messages, winner spreadsheet, and publisher contact information.
- Tools: Social media inbox, Google Sheets, email.
- Inputs: Winner announcement, winner names, submitted emails, and publisher contact.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is select “Messages” and click “Message Requests”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the communication step before sending or recording outreach; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Messages. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Then, click “Accept” to accept the message request of the person.

    Note: Before you accept the message request of the person, make sure to double-check if he/she really won the giveaway. His/her name should be the same as the one you tweeted on the announcement.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the communication step before sending or recording outreach; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: After confirming that he/she is the winner, copy his/her email and paste it into the [DataTalks.Club’s spreadsheet book of the week winner](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit#gid=805910618).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled his/her email and paste it into the DataTalks. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Don’t forget to reply to the winner of the book.

    Note: After you collected all the emails of the winners, send them to the [book publisher’s contact](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PBO4QJdNkh6n7oqvLT_Eimwum2amuL1hVJef_lkYzCE/edit?usp=sharing) via email.

frontmatter.title: Create a podcast document
frontmatter.summary: Steps to create a podcast planning document with event information, guest questions, and announcement details.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Create a podcast document.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: We need to create a podcast document that will contain the event information as well as the questions for the guest.
- Outcome: This is a communication tool between us, the guests, and people who help us prepare questions. We also use it for announcing the event.
- Trigger: Right after the guest confirmed the date for the podcast.
- Frequency: Per confirmed podcast event.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Podcast Google Drive folder, podcast template, schedule spreadsheet, and calendar.
- Tools: Google Drive, Google Docs, Google Sheets, Google Calendar.
- Inputs: Guest name, topic, event date, guest links, bio, outline, questions, and materials.

flat_steps.body_md: First, open the [Podcast](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1NEm9zmr8W2zks5WIn0SOQMr6tWgZvy8D) folder in the Files drive

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Podcast folder in the Files drive. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Once you are in the podcast folder, select the template and click “Make a copy”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Make a copy. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, rename the title of the template.

    The format:

    Date of the event - Name of guest - Topic

    e.g. 2022-02-11 - Jakob Graff - A/B Testing

    If the title is not yet known, put “TBA” for topic – and later update it

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled TBA. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Then, edit the document. This includes the name of the guest, the topic, the subtitle, the introduction, and the outline of the event.
    Replace the following placeholders:

    - \<NAME\> — full name of the speaker

    - \<FIRST NAME\> — the first name of the speaker

    - Links for Twitter, LinkedIn, Github, and Website

    When adding the questions to the document, use the “Enter” key for linebreaks, not “Ctrl+ENTER”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Enter. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Don’t forget to add the materials from the guest at the bottom part of page.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: The next thing to do is share the document with the guest speaker. Click on the "Share" button on the upper right of your screen.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Share. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
And, add the email of the guest and click "Send". Don't forget to make the guest an "Editor"

    Make sure that to also share the podcast document with anyone with the link

    Add Johanna’s email as edit in the document

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Johanna’s email as edit in the document. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Johanna’s email as edit in the document. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Afterward, send the link of the document to the speaker:

    Follow this [Podcast - Sending the podcast link to the speaker](../templates/podcast-adding-johanna-and-sending-the-podcast-link-to-the-speaker.md) template.

    And add Johanna to the email thread: Johanna Bayer \<johanna@datatalks.club\>

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the communication step before sending or recording outreach; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Johanna to the email thread. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Next, add the link to the podcast document in the trello card.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: To insert the document link to the podcast cell, open the [DataTaks.Club schedule spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit#gid=0).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled DataTaks. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, select the cell with the podcast event

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled cell with the podcast event. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, click “Insert” and press “Link” (or click Ctrl+K)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Insert. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: After, paste the podcast document link and click “Apply”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Apply. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

prose.body_md: Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Creating Podcast Transcript Summary with ChatGPT
frontmatter.summary: Steps to use ChatGPT to summarize podcast transcript sections, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Creating Podcast Transcript Summary with ChatGPT.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Use ChatGPT to summarize podcast transcript sections.
- Outcome: A cleaned summary is ready for the podcast page or related publishing workflow.
- Trigger: A podcast transcript is available and needs a summary.
- Frequency: Per podcast transcript.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: ChatGPT and the podcast transcript document.
- Tools: ChatGPT, Google Docs.
- Inputs: Podcast transcript sections and summary prompt.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open the website [ChatGPT](https://chat.openai.com/chat).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the communication step before sending or recording outreach; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled website ChatGPT. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
And then enter this text on the chat box,

    ```text
    You're a podcast producer and helping with summarizing the transcript of a podcast. I'll give you a piece of transcript and you'll give me back a bulleted list with a few main points. Ok?

    =======

    Give a list with two main points from this text:

    \<TEXT\>
    ```

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: After, copy one section in the podcast transcription and paste into the text box.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Once the AI replied, copy the reply and reserve it for later.

    Note: Do it for the rest of the sections of the podcast transcription.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Once the AI is done generating all of the sections of the podcast, copy the generated text and add this prompt

    ```text
    Here is the list of all the points you extracted

    Now make it shorter (at most 10) and more concise:

    \<GENERATED TEXTS OF THE AI\>
    ```

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

How to Create Podcast Transcript Summary with ChatGPT
This procedure will show you the steps on how to Create Podcast Transcript Summary with ChatGPT.

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Creating podcast transcription document
frontmatter.summary: Steps to use an automated tool to transcribe podcast episodes, generate transcripts, and edit them using ChatGPT, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Creating podcast transcription document.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Use an automated tool to transcribe podcast episodes, generate transcripts, and edit them using ChatGPT.
- Outcome: We need transcripts for our podcast episodes – we publish them on our website. The tool we describe here makes the process simple.
- Trigger: a new podcast episode needs to be transcribed and edited for creating a docx file and publishing on our website.
- Frequency: Per podcast episode that needs a transcript.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: YouTube Studio, Google Drive podcast audio folder, transcription tool, and ChatGPT.
- Tools: YouTube Studio, VLC, Google Drive, transcription tool, ChatGPT.
- Inputs: YouTube video ID, downloaded video, extracted audio, podcast title, and transcript text.

steps.body_md:
Open the Youtube video for the podcast (You can find it in the trello board) and click “Edit video” under the video

    You can also skip this step by putting the YouTube ID for the video to this link: [https://studio.youtube.com/video/YOUTUBE_ID/edit](https://studio.youtube.com/video/YOUTUBE_ID/edit) (replace YOUTUBE_ID with the actual ID)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Take a note of the YouTube video ID - you will need it throughout this task
Click on the 3 dots and select “Download” to download the video

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Download. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Use VLC to extract the audio track from the video: TODO

Upload the audio to the [podcast-raw-audio folder](https://drive.google.com/drive/u/1/folders/1lk5r7T1Ggs54lf0KI4_0bY5WQVkjuczT) in the Files Google Drive

    For that, you can drag-and-drop the file or right click on the empty space and select “File Upload”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled File Upload. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Open the file by double-clicking on it

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled file by double-clicking on it. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Click on the “Share” button in the right top corner

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Share. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Click on “General Access” (dropdown list with “Restricted”) and select “Anyone with the link”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled General Access. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Then click “Copy Link”


    You will get a link that looks like that:

    [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1cRh8yiLURL-xXNke2p0T7FaccOumF1YV/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1cRh8yiLURL-xXNke2p0T7FaccOumF1YV/view?usp=sharing)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Copy Link. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Note the File ID: it’s the content between /d/ and /view
    For link

    [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1cRh8yiLURL-xXNke2p0T7FaccOumF1YV/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1cRh8yiLURL-xXNke2p0T7FaccOumF1YV/view?usp=sharing)

    The File ID is

    1cRh8yiLURL-xXNke2p0T7FaccOumF1YV

    11lnh2GoZwCyEU-O1d2rq2vBOZHxDp8Y0

    11lnh2GoZwCyEU-O1d2rq2vBOZHxDp8Y0
    11lnh2GoZwCyEU-O1d2rq2vBOZHxDp8Y0

    11lnh2GoZwCyEU-O1d2rq2vBOZHxDp8Y0

    Now we’re ready to submit a transcription job.

    Go to [https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/)
Access GitHub Actions. Go to the GitHub repository and click on the "Actions" tab.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Actions. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: There are multiple actions available: Click on the "Submit Drive MP3 Transaction Job" action.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Submit Drive MP3 Transaction Job. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Submit a Transcription Job. Click on "Run workflow" under the "Submit Transcribe Job" action.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Run workflow. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Enter the YouTube video ID and the File ID (from Google drive) to the form and click “Run workflow”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the correct record, field, or status before updating the workflow; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Run workflow. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
You will see the submitted job is running


    Wait till it’s successful:

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Now we submitted a transcription job. It typically requires 10-15 minutes to complete, so we need to wait.

After 10-15 minutes are over, we can check the status of the transcription job, and if it’s ready, pull the results.

    For that, let’s navigate to the “Check Transcribe Jobs” action

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Check Transcribe Jobs. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Trigger it by clicking “Run workflow”


    And then clicking “Run workflow

    Give it 10-20 seconds to finish

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Run workflow. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Go to the transcriber repository ([https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/)).

    If the job is completed, you will see “now” (or a few minutes ago – depending on how much time you waited) in the “transcript/raw” folder

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled now. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Go to this [“transcripts/raw”](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/tree/main/transcripts/raw) folder, and open the file with our YouTube ID by clicking on it

    It should have “Now” or a few minutes ago in the “Last commit date” column

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Now. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Keep the tab with the content open. We will later use it

Go to Google Drive, go to the Files drive, the transcripts folder

    [https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1khibztKmYTdyMBRjaQeiaNHXuE0A2HUw](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1khibztKmYTdyMBRjaQeiaNHXuE0A2HUw)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Google Drive. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Create a new Google Document. Click “New”


    And then “Google Docs”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled New. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Google Docs. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Rename it (File → Rename)

    The name should follow this pattern:

    s10e07-short-name, where s10 is the podcast season, e07 is the episode number

    You can find the season and the episode in the [DataTalks.Club schedule document](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit), columns S and T:

    So, for “Using Data to Create Liveable Cities - Rachel Lim”, it’ll be “s19e01-livable-cities”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled document. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Keep the tab with this document open, we will need it later

Open ChatGPT ([https://chatgpt.com/](https://chatgpt.com/))

    Note: Make sure you use ChatGPT 4o, not 4o-mini

There’s a prompts.md file in the transcriber repository. We need the “Prompt for correcting” from there. It’s accessible using this link:

    [https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/blob/main/prompt.md#prompt-for-correcting](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/blob/main/prompt.md#prompt-for-correcting)

    Copy this prompt to your clipboard. You can either manually select the prompt or click on the “copy” icon

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled copy. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Paste the prompt to ChatGPT, but don’t execute it yet. We need to replace a few placeholders there. We have two: {GUEST_NAME} and {QUESTIONS}

    First, replace the placeholder “{GUEST_NAME}” with the first name of the guest. E.g. for “Rachel Lim” it’s “Rachel”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled {GUESTNAME}. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Next, we need to replace the {QUESTIONS} placeholder.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Locate the podcast document for this particular podcast interview, copy the entire content of this document to the clipboard and paste it replacing the {QUESTIONS} placeholder

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Click on Run button


    It will acknowledge the task and ask for the transcript.(The exact wording may be different)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Run button. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Now let’s copy the transcript into ChatGPT for editing. We will do it in chunks

    First, copy roughly 20 minutes of the transcript and make sure that the last phrase is from the guest (unless it’s the last phrase of the transcript)

    And paste to ChatGPT. Click Run

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled ChatGPT. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Sometimes it will stop midway. In this case, click “Continue generating”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Continue generating. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Select the output and copy it to the clipboard


    Paste it to the Google document with the transcript

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled output and copy it to the clipboard. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled it to the Google document with the transcript. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Continue this with chunks of ~20 minutes each until the entire transcript is edited with ChatGPT and copied to the Google document. Typically you’ll need to do it 3 times: 0-20, 20-40 and 40-60 minutes

The document is ready and we need to do a few manual edits. First, we remove the introduction part
    It’s usually the same in all our podcast interviews – it’s all the content before “This week, we’ll talk about …”

    So remove the intro:

    And if the first remaining paragraph contains things before “this week we’ll” that are irrelevant to the interview, remove them too:

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled this week we’ll. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Finally, add spaces around dashes.

    Press Ctrl+H (or click Edit → Search and replace) and put (without quotes)

    Find: “(?\<!\s)—(?!\s)”
    Replace: “ — “
    Check “Use regular expressions”


    Then click “Replace all”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Use regular expressions. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Now we will use ChatGPT again to add sections.

    We need the “Prompt for titles” from the prompts.md file. It’s accessible using this link:

    [https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/blob/main/prompt.md#prompt-for-titles](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/blob/main/prompt.md#prompt-for-titles)

    Copy this prompt to your clipboard. You can either manually select the prompt or click on the “copy” icon

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled copy. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Run the prompt. It will respond with acknowledging the task

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Copy the entire transcript to ChatGPT and click Run

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled entire transcript to ChatGPT and click Run. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled entire transcript to ChatGPT and click Run. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Wait till it finishes. You’ll get output that looks like this:

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Now we need to put each chapter back to the transcript document.

    The first chapter is easy, so we will take the second one as the example:

    2:52 Rachel's career journey: from geography to urban data science
    Find the “2:52” timestamp in the document


    Put the name “Rachel's career journey: from geography to urban data science” right before this timestamp (Don’t include the timestamp):


    Make it a header 2: select the text and press Ctrl+Alt+2:

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled 2:52. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled text and press Ctrl+Alt+2. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Sometimes the timestamps aren’t correct. Often this happens when the timestamp points to the answer, not a question

    For example, for “30:09 Data analysis for transportation policies”, we’d put the title at the 30:09 timestamp:

    But it’s the answer to the question that is asked before 30:09. So we need to move it to the question:

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Repeat it for all the headers.
The document is ready! Put the link to it to the trello card.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
We can now download as docx:

    File → Download → Microsoft Word (.docx)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled → Microsoft Word (. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: We can now use it for other tasks – TODO

Delete the audio file from the drive.

    Go to the “Files \> podcast-raw-audio” folder:

    [https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1lk5r7T1Ggs54lf0KI4_0bY5WQVkjuczT](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1lk5r7T1Ggs54lf0KI4_0bY5WQVkjuczT)

    And delete the file we uploaded earlier:

    right click on the file → Move to trash

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled the file → Move to trash. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Move the raw transcript we created on github

    Go to the “transcripts/raw” folder in the transcriber github repository:

    [https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/tree/main/transcripts/raw](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/podcast-transcriber/tree/main/transcripts/raw)

    And open the transcript (you should still have this tab open if you haven’t closed it)

    Type “processed/” before the file name

    Once you add “/” after “processed”, it becomes a directory:

    Now click “Commit changes”

    And confirm the commit by clicking “Commit changes”:

    The file should be now in the “processed” folder:

    Loom links:

frontmatter.title: Editing Podcast with Audacity
frontmatter.summary: Steps to combine host and guest podcast audio in Audacity and normalize the final track for publishing.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Editing Podcast with Audacity.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: To combine the host and guest audio into one track and normalize the sound.
- Outcome: A combined and normalized podcast audio file is ready for publishing.
- Trigger: Raw host and guest podcast recordings are available.
- Frequency: Per podcast recording.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Podcast Dropbox folder and local Audacity installation.
- Tools: Audacity, Dropbox.
- Inputs: Host and guest audio tracks, start point, and target export format.

flat_steps.body_md:
Download and install the Audacity application on your device. Open Audacity once installed.

    [https://www.audacityteam.org/download](https://www.audacityteam.org/download)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled install the Audacity application on your device. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Go to [podcast dropbox](https://www.dropbox.com/home/podcast-new?di=left_nav_browse) and select the podcast audio that you will edit. Download the audio from the Host (Alexey) and the Guest.

    Note: We usually have two recordings (sometimes more). Each speaker has their own individual track, so the files are not merged yet. The reason for multiple tracks is that different microphones may capture sound differently. That’s why we later normalize the files separately to level the sound.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Click and drag the downloaded audio file from your File Manager into the Audacity application.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Locate the exact spot where Alexey (host) starts speaking “This week we talked about…” usually between 5–10 minutes.Do the same for the guest’s track.

    Note: Note: You can use the zoom-in option to make this easier to see.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on both audio tracks, then hold Shift on your keyboard and drag to select the portion before the identified cut point. Finally, press Delete on your keyboard to remove that section from both tracks.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled both audio tracks. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Play the audio to check if the cut was successful, it should now start with “This week we talked about…”.
To normalize the volume, double-click on the Host audio track to select it, then click “Effect” in the menu bar. Go to “Volume and Compression” then“Loudness Normalization”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Effect. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
In the pop-up window, make sure LUFS is set to -16, then click “OK”.


    It may take some time to complete the process.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled OK. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Do the same for the Guest audio track, follow steps 7–9, then proceed to step 11.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Play the audio to check if both tracks start correctly and the volume is balanced.
Download the [podcast intro from dropbox](https://www.dropbox.com/home/podcast-new?preview=Podcast+intro+(8s).mp3)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled podcast intro from dropbox. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Click and drag the downloaded audio file from your File Manager into the Audacity application.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Click and drag Alexey’s audio after the podcast intro and do it to the guests audio as well.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Play to check the audio.
Click “File” in the menu bar, hover over “Export” in the dropdown, then select “Export as MP3.”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled File. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Rename the file using the format: YYYY-MM-DD-guestsfirstname-lastname. Then click on “Save”.

    Example: 2025-08-18-pastor-soto

    Note: Use the podcast recording date (when the podcast happened), not the date you exported the file.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on “OK” at the pop up window.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled OK. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Then click on “OK” again at the pop up window.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled OK. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

prose.body_md:
Editing Podcast with Audacity
This document shows the steps to editing podcasts with Audacity..

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Find speaker's link in Google and pasting into the podcast document
frontmatter.summary: Steps to find speaker links online and add them to the podcast document, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Find speaker_s link in Google and pasting into the podcast document.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Find speaker links online and add them to the podcast document.
- Outcome: The podcast document includes the speaker social and website links that can be found.
- Trigger: A podcast document needs guest links.
- Frequency: Per podcast guest.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Google search and the podcast document.
- Tools: Google, browser, Google Docs.
- Inputs: Guest name, target link types, and podcast document link.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you need to do is open [Google.com](https://www.google.com/) and search for the name of the guest.

    Note: In this example, the name of the guest is “Mısra Turp” You can also specify what contact details you want to find.

    For example: \< Name of the Guest \> \< Social Media platform \>
    \< Mısra Turp \> \< LinkedIn\>

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: After searching for the name of the guest, you can now click his/her links. This includes her LinkedIn, Twitter, Github, and website.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled his/her links. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: In this example, copy the LinkedIn link

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled LinkedIn link. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
And then, paste the copied link into the podcast document.

    Note: This is also true for other links (Twitter, Github, etc…) If you can’t find his/her contact details, you can ask for them through email.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

This procedure will show you the steps on how to find the speaker’s link in Google.

frontmatter.title: Fixing a Typo in the Podcast Transcript
frontmatter.summary: Steps to correct a typo in a published podcast transcript, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Fixing a Typo in the Podcast Transcript.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Correct a typo in a published podcast transcript.
- Outcome: The podcast transcript page is updated and committed in GitHub.
- Trigger: A typo or incorrect spelling is found in a podcast transcript.
- Frequency: As needed.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalksClub website GitHub repository.
- Tools: GitHub editor, browser find and replace.
- Inputs: Podcast page, incorrect text, and replacement text.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you need to do is visit DataTalksClub’ github repository, and select “podcast”

    Note: You can also visit this* [*link*](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/datatalksclub.github.io/tree/main/_podcast) *directly.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, navigate and click the podcast episode you want to change or update.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled podcast episode you want to change or update. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click the pen icon to edit the podcast episode.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled pen icon to edit the podcast episode. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Once done, press “Ctrl +F” on your keyboard and add the word on the “Find” section, and the word you want to chage/replace in the “Replace” section. After, click “Replace All” to update the word.

    Note: In this example, we want to change “Maryam” to “Meryem”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Maryam. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Finally, click “Commit changes” to save the update you’ve made on the repo.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Commit changes. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

frontmatter.title: Generate Timecodes from docx Transcriptions
frontmatter.summary: Steps for generating timecodes from a document with transcriptions, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Generate Timecodes from docx Transcriptions.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: generating timecodes from a document with transcriptions
- Outcome: Podcast timecodes are generated from the transcript document.
- Trigger: after the the transcript document is created
- Frequency: Per podcast transcript that needs timecodes.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Transcript Google Drive folder and transcript-utils GitHub repository.
- Tools: Google Drive, GitHub, transcript-utils.
- Inputs: Podcast transcript docx file, episode title, and season or episode identifier.

TODO:

- Move the anchor.fm part in a separate document and update it for Spotify for Podcasters. In the new document, link this one

steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open the transcript document – it should be in the trello card and in the [transcripts](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1khibztKmYTdyMBRjaQeiaNHXuE0A2HUw) folder.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Download the transcript, click on "File" and click "Download- Microsoft word (.docx)”\]

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled File. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
If relevant, rename the document.
    Note: when renaming, you must follow the format: "SeasonEpisode-Title”,

    e.g “s07e07-ab-tests”. There should be no spaces in between the words and make all letters lowercase and don’t add “-” at the end.

    Incorrect file name:

    - s08e09-From-Academia-to-Data-Analytics-and-Engineering-.docx

    - S08e09- From-Academia-to-Data- Analytics-and- Engineering-.docx

    Correct filename:

    - s08e09-from-academia-to-data-analytics-and-engineering.docx

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: To proceed, open [transcript-utils/docs](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/transcript-utils/tree/main) on GitHub

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled transcript-utils/docs on GitHub. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: On the top right of your screen, click "Add file" and select "Upload files"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Add file. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: And then, click "choose your files" or you may drag the file.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled choose your files. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
After dragging or selecting your file, click "Commit changes"

    Note: Wait for a few seconds before the uploading will be completed. To check the progress, click on "Actions”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Actions. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: And after the file has been uploaded, click on "transcripts"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled transcripts. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Once done, the uploaded transcript will appear. Click on the transcript

    Note: In this example, the name of the transcript is "s07s06-ab-test.txt"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled s07s06-ab-test.txt. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md:
Then, copy the transcript with the timecodes provided.


    Note that it also creates the transcript – see [Adding Transcripts to Podcast Episodes](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wMjTuAtHWf4ibuqqUyPs0kgJqio1Acq4BvbqknKCJC0/edit) to add it to the website

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled transcript with the timecodes provided. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Afterward, go to DataTalks.Club's YouTube channel and select the podcast video.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled DataTalks. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Click on "Edit Video" under the YouTube video beside "Analytics"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Edit Video. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: And then, paste the timecodes of the podcast event

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled timecodes of the podcast event. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: To add the outline on the Anchor podcast, open [anchor. fm](https://anchor.fm/) and click on "Episodes"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Episodes. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: After, click the podcast episode.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled podcast episode. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Once you are in the podcast episode, tap on the pen tool icon.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: After tapping, you can now paste the outline of the podcast and click

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled outline of the podcast and click. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: After reviewing the necessary changes and edits, click on "Update episode"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Update episode. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

frontmatter.title: Generating Youtube Timestamp via Notebook
frontmatter.summary: Steps to generate YouTube timestamps from a video transcript using NotebookLM, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Generating Youtube Timestamp via Notebook.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Generate YouTube timestamps from a video transcript using NotebookLM.
- Outcome: A concise timestamp list is ready for the YouTube description.
- Trigger: A YouTube video needs timestamps and a description.
- Frequency: Per YouTube video.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: NotebookLM, YouTube video transcript, and description template.
- Tools: NotebookLM, YouTube, Google Docs template.
- Inputs: YouTube transcript, target video, prompt, and description template.

flat_steps.body_md:
Go to [https://notebooklm.google.com/](https://notebooklm.google.com/). Click “Create new notebook”.

    Note: You will be automatically logged in to your email account.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the communication step before sending or recording outreach; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Go to youtube, select the video that you will need to add timestamp and description into.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled youtube. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Scroll down and click on “Show transcript”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Show transcript. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Select all the generated timestamp from youtube and copy.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled all the generated timestamp from youtube and copy. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Go back to Notebook and click on “Copied text”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Copied text. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Paste the timecodes. Then click on “Insert”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Insert. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
When the source is ready, type in the field “Generate timestamp, max 20” and click on “Enter”.
    Go to this [template](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ssz02t9SBQ5pCXeF8IjDCqKjhgqoNIa5kD8PWSndEAw/edit?tab=t.0), and see the prompts.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled this template. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Copy the generated timestamp

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled generated timestamp. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Go to ChatGpt, open a new chat and copy the prompt from this template [Template for YouTube descriptions](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ssz02t9SBQ5pCXeF8IjDCqKjhgqoNIa5kD8PWSndEAw/edit?tab=t.0) then paste the timestamp right after the prompt. Click enter.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled ChatGpt. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Copy the final timestamp.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled final timestamp. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Paste it in the youtube description.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled it in the youtube description. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

frontmatter.title: How to send a recorded podcast episode to a freelancer for a transcription
frontmatter.summary: Steps to send recorded podcast files to the transcription freelancer, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/How to send a recorded podcast episode to a freelancer for a transcription.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Send recorded podcast files to the transcription freelancer.
- Outcome: The freelancer receives Dropbox and YouTube links for transcription.
- Trigger: A podcast recording is edited and ready for transcription.
- Frequency: Per podcast episode needing transcription.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Podcast Dropbox folder, YouTube link, and email.
- Tools: Dropbox, email, YouTube.
- Inputs: Podcast Dropbox link, YouTube link, freelancer email, and email template.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open the [Dropbox podcast folder](https://www.dropbox.com/home/podcast).

frontmatter.title: Making Corrections to the already uploaded podcast document to Airtable
frontmatter.summary: Steps to correct a podcast transcript document after the website update workflow fails because the uploaded document has formatting or content issues, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Airtable/Making Corrections to the already uploaded podcast document to Airtable.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Correct a podcast transcript document after the website update workflow fails because the uploaded document has formatting or content issues.
- Outcome: The corrected transcript document is re-uploaded to Airtable and the GitHub workflow can be rerun.
- Trigger: The `generate_all` GitHub workflow fails after a podcast document was uploaded to Airtable.
- Frequency: Whenever an uploaded podcast transcript causes a website update workflow error.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: GitHub Actions, the podcast transcript folder, Airtable DTC workspace, and the podcast table.
- Tools: GitHub Actions, ChatGPT, Google Drive, Google Docs, Airtable.
- Inputs: Failed workflow error message, podcast transcript document, corrected `.docx` export, and the Airtable podcast record.

This process document will show you how to make corrections to the already uploaded podcast document to Airtable!

flat_steps.body_md: After running a [Github workflow](update-the-website-with-the-information-from-forms.md), and you encounter an error, click on the failed job to view the details.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the GitHub Actions workflow with a failed job. Opening the failed entry is the first step to finding which podcast transcript caused the website update error.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on 'generate_all' to view the workflow details and identify what went wrong.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the generate_all job in the workflow run. This job contains the logs needed to identify the transcript parsing or formatting failure.

flat_steps.body_md:
Copy the entire error message, open ChatGPT, and paste it to get an explanation of the issue.

    Note: In this error message, the issue is related to the document attached specifically, the podcast transcript.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the GitHub log output with the error message. Copying the full message preserves the file name and stack details needed for diagnosis.

flat_steps.body_md: Go to the [transcript folder](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1khibztKmYTdyMBRjaQeiaNHXuE0A2HUw), find and click on the podcast transcript with the error.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the transcript folder where the problematic podcast document is stored. Use the filename from the workflow error to open the matching transcript.

flat_steps.body_md:
Check the formatting and font. Ensure the following are correctly formatted. Go to the "[Creating Podcast Transcription](creating-podcast-transcription-document.md)" document and recheck the transcript to make sure it follows the process.

    Timecode Titles

    Use Heading 2 for section titles, and do not include timestamps in the title line.

    ✖️ 00:00 Orell’s career and move to freelancing

    ✅ Orell’s career and move to freelancing

    Timestamps

    Speaker Name

    Transcript Content

    Note: Sometimes, ChatGPT may not follow the prompt correctly while generating the transcript. Make sure to double-check the output and ensure the formatting is followed. If needed, you can redo the transcript to meet the required format.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the transcript document with headings, timestamps, speaker names, and transcript text. It illustrates the formatting checks needed before exporting the corrected file.

flat_steps.body_md: Once done, click on “File,” then “Download,” and select “Microsoft Word (.docx).”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Google Docs download menu with Microsoft Word (.docx) selected. Exporting this format creates the replacement file to upload back into Airtable.

flat_steps.body_md:
Go and log into [Airtable.](https://airtable.com/) Click on “DTC org.”

    Note: If you don’t see it at the top, scroll down through the workspace list, it’s sorted by most recently opened, so it might be further down.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Airtable workspace list with DTC org selected. This confirms you are entering the shared base that stores podcast submissions.

flat_steps.body_md: Select the “podcast” Tab.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the podcast table tab in Airtable. Selecting it narrows the records to podcast entries and their transcript attachments.

flat_steps.body_md: Scroll down to find the podcast entry with the submitted form that has an error. Once located, use the horizontal scroll bar at the bottom to drag right and view more details in that row.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the podcast table row for the submitted form and the horizontal scroll area. It helps locate the record with the transcript attachment that needs replacement.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on the existing transcript.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the existing transcript attachment in the Airtable record. Opening it lets you manage the file before uploading the corrected version.

flat_steps.body_md: Remove the attachment by clicking the trash bin or delete icon.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the attachment preview with the delete control. Removing the old file prevents Airtable from keeping the transcript version that caused the workflow failure.

flat_steps.body_md: Click the attachment icon.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the empty or editable attachment field in the Airtable podcast record. The attachment icon is where the corrected transcript upload begins.

flat_steps.body_md: Click "browse files”or drag the transcript Word document from your file manager into the field.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows Airtable's file upload dialog with the browse files option. Use it to attach the corrected .docx transcript to the podcast record.

flat_steps.body_md:
After replacing the transcript document, go back to [GitHub](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/datatalksclub.github.io/actions) and click on "Re-run jobs", then select "Re-run all jobs".

    Note: It's best to wait a few minutes before re-running the GitHub workflow. This allows the system to register the updated transcript and ensures any background changes are fully saved before triggering the job again.


    Note: If the workflow shows a ✅ green check mark, it means everything ran successfully and no further action is needed.

    However, if it shows a ❌ error again, repeat the process from Step 1.
    If the error persists or you're unsure how to proceed, feel free to ping Alexey on Telegram to let him know and to ask for assistance.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows GitHub Actions with the Re-run jobs menu open. Re-running all jobs checks whether the corrected Airtable attachment resolves the workflow error.
The screenshot shows the workflow status after rerunning the job. Use the green check or repeated failure state to decide whether the correction is complete or needs escalation.

frontmatter.title: Making event announcements when topic, bio, or outline is missing
frontmatter.summary: Steps to announce a podcast event even when the topic, bio, or outline is incomplete, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Making event announcements when topic, bio, or outline is missing.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Announce a podcast event even when the topic, bio, or outline is incomplete.
- Outcome: The event is published with placeholders or available guest information.
- Trigger: A podcast event needs announcement before all details are ready.
- Frequency: As needed for podcast announcements.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Luma and event announcement templates.
- Tools: Luma, Google Docs templates.
- Inputs: Event title, date and time, available topic, bio, outline, and placeholder text.

flat_steps.body_md: If the Outline is missing: Enter "TBA" under the Outline: section.

If the Speaker Bio is missing:

    - Step 1: Check their LinkedIn "About" or "Description" section and use that as the bio.

    - Step 2: If no LinkedIn description exists, use Gemini or ChatGPT to generate a 2-3 sentence bio based on their current job title, company, and past experience.

    - Final Notification Step: Once the announcement is live, notify the guest or sponsor immediately.

    Example:

    Hi Elizaveta and Ernst,

    Hope you are both doing great!

    We have announced the event as we want to start promoting it as early as possible. Let us know when you have the bio and we will update it in the description.

    Here is the link: [https://luma.com/f6zc5lr6](https://luma.com/f6zc5lr6)

    Thank you!

prose.body_md:
Making event announcements when topic, bio, or outline is missing

This procedure will show you the steps on how to make a podcast event announcement if the outline is not ready, only the topic. This usually happens when the guest submitted the information (outline and bio) on the podcast document late.

Step-by-step Instructions
In Luma, follow the normal event creation process. If some information is missing, use the guide below:
