frontmatter.title: Schedule podcast episodes with Spotify for podcaster
frontmatter.summary: Steps to schedule a podcast episode in Spotify for Podcasters, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Podcast/Schedule podcast episodes with Spotify for podcaster.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Schedule a podcast episode in Spotify for Podcasters.
- Outcome: The edited podcast episode is uploaded and scheduled for release.
- Trigger: An edited podcast audio file is ready to publish.
- Frequency: Per podcast episode.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Spotify for Podcasters and podcast Dropbox folder.
- Tools: Spotify for Podcasters, Dropbox.
- Inputs: Edited podcast audio, title, description, cover image, and release date.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you need to do is login to [Spotify for podcaster](https://anchor.fm/dashboard/analytics). Select "New episode" on the left-side menu.

    Note: Alexey invited you to Spotify during onboarding.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: It will take you to the Upload screen below.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled screen below. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: On another tab, open the DataTalks.Club's dropbox and click the "podcast" folder.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled podcast. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Inside “podcast”, click "Edited".

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled podcast. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
In the "Edited" folder, select the podcast episode you want to post.

    Note: In this case, we’re uploading Kataryzna Foremni’s edited recording.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Hover over the file to reveal the three dots on the upper right. Click the three dots and select “Download”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Download. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Return to Spotify and click “Select a file” and upload the downloaded file in the previous step.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Select a file. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
On the “Details” step, you can now enter information about the podcast. This includes the episode title, episode description, publish date, season and episode number, and the links of the speaker and publish the podcast episode during summertime in Germany (Friday); it's 1:00 AM (Saturday) Philippines time, and otherwise, it's 2:00 AM.

    Note: You can get the title of the podcast from the YouTube video. The description includes the extracted timecodes from the transcript (can be seen [here](https://github.com/alexeygrigorev/transcript-utils/tree/main/timecodes)) and the links (Github repositories, LinkedIn profile, job listings, etc…) In addition, to get the correct link- you must be in editor mode to copy the link. Here’s [more information](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xMqwZfr3hiszCUzi3FUMilGTkq4OhrS5rW918IwN0J0/edit?usp=sharing).

    Description for this should be the timecodes from this doc:[Creating podcast transcription document](creating-podcast-transcription-document.md)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Scroll down and type in the Season Number and Episode number, check it [here](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit?gid=604757765#gid=604757765)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Once done, click "Schedule episode". Make sure it’s scheduled on a Friday at 19:00 Berlin time.

    Note: If there are multiple podcast, schedule only one episode every friday.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled only one episode every friday. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

prose.body_md:
This procedure will show you the steps on how to schedule podcast episodes with Anchor.

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Selecting the date and time for the event using our [DataTalks.Club schedule](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/u/1/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit) spreadsheet and [https://calendar.google.com/calendar](https://calendar.google.com/calendar)
- Outcome: We need to keep track of the dates in one place
- Trigger: When we already have the contact information of the guest (email) and need to select the date for the event
- Frequency: Per prospective event guest.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club schedule spreadsheet, Google Calendar, and guest email.
- Tools: Google Sheets, Google Calendar, email.
- Inputs: Guest contact information, availability, event type, and proposed date.

Note: the communication should happen via email, not linkedin

flat_steps.body_md: Find a suitable date from [DataTalks.Club schedule](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/u/1/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit). See [Schedule](../../../overview/reference/schedule.md) for more detail.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled more detail. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

And then

    - write "proposed" on the “status” column

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the correct record, field, or status before updating the workflow; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled proposed. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next, go to [Calendar](https://calendar.google.com/calendar/u/0/r/eventedit?state=%5Bnull%2Cnull%2Cnull%2Cnull%2C%5B13%5D%5D&pli=1) and propose the event. Follow this template [Create a calender invite for the guests speaker for an event](../../../events/calendar/sops/create-a-calender-invite-for-the-guests-speaker-for-an-event.md) but add “Proposed” in the beginning.

    Format - “Proposed - \<NAME\> at DataTalks.Club Podcast” - for podcast

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the correct record, field, or status before updating the workflow; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Proposed - <NAME> at DataTalks.Club Podcast. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Send the proposed date to the guest.
    Template: [Podcast process - First contact email, starting the process](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wdlY9mH-rMBKW5AjFY32tjc1zTRfi7ZjvU1SxqxbekY/edit)

flat_steps.body_md: If the speaker doesn’t confirm within one week, follow up.

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Add generated timecodes to a YouTube video description.
- Outcome: The YouTube description has formatted timecodes and is saved after review.
- Trigger: A video needs timecodes added to its description.
- Frequency: Per video.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club YouTube Studio, description template, and guest email.
- Tools: YouTube Studio, ChatGPT, Gmail.
- Inputs: Target video, generated timecodes, description template, and guest review notes.

flat_steps.body_md: Go to [How to Use YouTube Description Templates](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1nQQ0wXRuqqVJ5L4CL9xvkHnoAFDxBDld86sj3_LvZ5A/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.jq3jzf8zxz81)and follow the template for Open Source Spotlight
Next, open DataTalks.Club’s Youtube channel and click on “YouTube studio”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled YouTube studio. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click “content"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled content. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, click on the pen tool, right beside the video.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled the pen tool. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Under "Description", paste the template along with the ChatGPT generated timecodes of the video.

    Note: You only need the beginning of the time. For example, instead of "00:00 - 02:00" ,

    paste only "00:00" Moreover, make sure that the timecode should start at "00:00" Leading zeros to timecodes, and remove punctuations after the timestamp.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled 00:00. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Copy the Youtube link and ask the guest to review the timecodes through gmail using this template [Open-source spotlight Asking for revisions, links and timecodes](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_jJLDGSTuyRGz6fimgwJLBGyT_dVl_rfr8T50qIqwa8/edit?tab=t.0)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Lastly, make changes if needed and click "Save"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Save. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is go on YouTube studio, Click the DTC logo and select “YouTube Studio”

flat_steps.body_md: And then, go to “Content” and click “Live”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Content. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click the three-dotted button of the video

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled three-dotted button of the video. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: And click “Download”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Download. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
After it has been downloaded, on the top-right of your screen, click “Create” and select “Upload Videos”

    Note: In here, you’ll be uploading the view you;ve downloaded.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
Now, update the Title of the video and other updates to be done. Once done, click “Save”

    Note: Copy this format: \<TITLE OF THE VIDEO\> \[FIXED SOUND\]

    Make sure also to add the video on the paylist and update the cover.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled video on the paylist and update the cover. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Don’t forget to add “Public” as the video’s visibility. Once done, click “Publish”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the upload, publishing, or scheduling state before it becomes user-facing; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Public. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md:
For the old version, edit the title and add the link of the updated version on the description .

    Note: Format of the title: \[UPDATED VERSION IN THE DESCRIPTION\\ \<TITLE OF THE VIDEO\>

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the correct record, field, or status before updating the workflow; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, remove it from the playlist. After, click “Done”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Done. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: And, make it “Unlisted”. After, click “Done”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the process is on the expected screen before the next action; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Unlisted. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

flat_steps.body_md: Lastly, click “Save”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for confirming the download or export step is using the right option; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Save. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

frontmatter.title: Generate Timecodes Using Youtube Video Transcripts
frontmatter.summary: Steps to use YouTube transcripts and the description template to generate summaries, timestamps, and supporting links.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Youtube/Generate Timecodes Using Youtube Video Transcripts.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: How to use the [Template for YouTube descriptions](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ssz02t9SBQ5pCXeF8IjDCqKjhgqoNIa5kD8PWSndEAw/edit?tab=t.0) file template.
- Outcome: A YouTube description has a generated summary, timestamps, and supporting links.
- Trigger: Use this process document as a guide to use the template for adding Youtube Descriptions
- Frequency: Per video description update.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: YouTube transcript, description template, and ChatGPT.
- Tools: YouTube, ChatGPT, Google Docs template.
- Inputs: Video transcript, guest name, title, social links, resource links, and prompt.

groups.title: Generate Summary and Timestamps for Youtube Description using ChatGPT

steps.body_md: Go to [Template for YouTube descriptions](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ssz02t9SBQ5pCXeF8IjDCqKjhgqoNIa5kD8PWSndEAw/edit?tab=t.0) and select the appropriate template for the video. Copy and Paste the first sentence for description and input \[GUEST NAME AND SURNAME\] about \[TITLE\] but for the \[SUMMARY\] and \[TIMESTAMPS\]. ChatGPT would be used to generate it.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Template for YouTube descriptions and select the approp. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Once you select your video on Youtube, scroll down to the description and select “Show Transcript”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for checking the editing, transcript, or timestamp workflow at this point; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled Show Transcript. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: A tab on the right side showing the transcript will appear and proceed to copy the transcript.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled transcript. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: Go back to the template and scroll down and look for the Prompt. Paste your transcript on the “\[paste transcript here\]” and then copy the prompt into ChatGPT.

screenshots.caption:
This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled paste transcript here. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.
    These prompts would be used for the timestamps and summaries.

steps.body_md: For the other details on the description, for the social media links if you're able to find it then add the \[LINK\], but if not then omit this part.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled LINK. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: For this section, just copy it.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or visible control labeled it. Use that match to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

steps.body_md: For this section, if the guest mentioned resources then add \[LINK\] here but if not then omit this part.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot matters for capturing or placing the correct link information; look for the highlighted area or matching UI state shown in the image. Use it to verify the screen state, then complete the step described above.

frontmatter.title: Cutting Videos with FFmpeg
frontmatter.summary: Steps to cut a video into multiple sections, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Other Process Documents/Cutting Videos with FFmpeg.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Cut a video into multiple sections.
- Outcome: To extract and reuse the most interesting parts of event recordings, podcasts, workshops, webinars, or courses. These clips can then be shared on social media to increase engagement and promote the full content.
- Trigger: A recording has interesting segments that should be reused as clips.
- Frequency: As needed for events, podcasts, workshops, webinars, or courses.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Source video and video chunks Drive folder.
- Tools: Notebook or Gemini for timestamps, Google Drive, and FFmpeg.
- Inputs: Source video, selected timestamps, and clip output folder.

flat_steps.body_md:
After getting the timestamp using notebooks or Gemini, type in the prompt:

    ```text
    List me the most interesting and insightful parts from this video.

    Output as start timestamp - end timestamp short summary

    Each part should be no longer than 5 minutes
    ```
Go to [video_chunks](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1zzaoiWqDNbVoRx0Vc-Hw7zWs5uCHrgDf). Click on “New”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for go to video chunks. Click on "New". Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Click “New Folder”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 3 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for click "New Folder". Look for the red box or arrow around "New Folder", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Rename it as the YYYY-MM-DD-Title of the video, then click “create”.
    In this example 2025-11-04 Building with MCP

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 4 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for rename it as the YYYY MM DD Title of the video, then click "create". In this example 2025 11 04 Building with MCP. Look for the red box or arrow around "create", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: In the folder, create a new document and paste the insightful parts generated in the Notebook. Rename the document as the title of the video.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for in the folder, create a new document and paste the insightful parts generated in the Notebook. Rename the document. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Scroll down, put the timestamp and the title of each insightful part and replace the spaces with underscores.
    NOTE: Remove all symbols (like &, ?, :, or -)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 6 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for scroll down, put the timestamp and the title of each insightful part and replace the spaces with underscores. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Go to your File Manager and create a new folder for the task and rename it as “video_edit”

    Example C:\Users\\YourName\>\Downloads\video_edit.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 7 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for go to your File Manager and create a new folder for the task and rename it as "video edit" Example. Look for the red box or arrow around "video edit", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Download the Video from Youtube.
    NOTE: Make sure that you are in YouTube Studio so you can easily download the original video file.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 8 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for download the Video from Youtube. Look for the red box or arrow around Download, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Move the downloaded source video into that “video_edit” folder and rename it to a simple name without spaces.

    Format: Youtube_Title.mp4.

    Or in this example is “Building_with_MCP”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 9 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for move the downloaded source video into that "video edit" folder and rename it to a simple name without spaces. Look for the red boxes or arrows around "video edit", "Building with MCP", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Go to [video_chunks](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1zzaoiWqDNbVoRx0Vc-Hw7zWs5uCHrgDf), download the FFmpeg file.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 10 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for go to video chunks, download the FFmpeg file. Look for the red box or arrow around Download, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Move ffmpeg.exe into your video_edit folder so it sits next to the video.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 11 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for move ffmpeg.exe into your video edit folder so it sits next to the video. Look for the red box or arrow around Next, Edit, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: In the Download folder copy the folder location.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 12 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for in the Download folder copy the folder location. Look for the red box or arrow around Download, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Go to Windows and type in Command Prompt and click it to open.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 13 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for go to Windows and type in Command Prompt and click it to open. Look for the red box or arrow around Open, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
In Command Prompt type: “cd” then paste the folder location C:\Users\\YourName\>\Downloads\video_edit then press Enter.
    cd C:\Users\Admin\Downloads\video_edit

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 14 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for in Command Prompt type: "cd" then paste the folder location C:\Users\\YourName\ \Downloads\video edit then press. Look for the red box or arrow around Download, Edit, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Type in “dir” and press Enter. It will verify that FFmpeg and the video are available in the folder.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 15 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for type in "dir" and press Enter. It will verify that FFmpeg and the video are available in the folder. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
To ensure the command works in the terminal, you must replace the placeholders in the Command with your specific data.

    Command: ffmpeg -ss 00:MM:SS -to 00:MM:SS -i input.mp4 -c copy chunk_N.mp4
    Rules for Manual Formatting:
    - Time: Use 00:MM:SS. If the transcript says 2:43, write 00:02:43.

    - Input: Replace input.mp4 with the exact title of your video (Building_with_MCP.mp4).

    - Output: Replace chunk_N.mp4 with the segment title.

    NOTE: Use underscores (\_) instead of spaces and remove all symbols (like &, ?, :, or -) as they will cause the command to fail.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 16 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for to ensure the command works in the terminal, you must replace the placeholders in the Command with your specific. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Go back to CMD and paste the command for the first segment, then press Enter.
    NOTE: Wait for the process to finish. Once the command line is ready for input again, repeat the process for the next segment until all are completed.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 17 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for go back to CMD and paste the command for the first segment, then press Enter. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Once all the videos are done, upload them from the “video_edit” folder on your desktop to the designated Google Drive folder.

    NOTE: Verify that all extracted clips are present in your local folder before initiating the transfer to ensure the Google Drive folder is complete.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 18 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for once all the videos are done, upload them from the "video edit" folder on your desktop to the designated Google. Look for the red box or arrow around "video edit", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

This screenshot anchors step 18 of the Cutting Videos with FFmpeg process by showing the screen for once all the videos are done, upload them from the "video edit" folder on your desktop to the designated Google. Look for the red box or arrow around "video edit", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Copy the Google folder link and send it to DTC content team in telegram

prose.body_md:
Cutting Videos with FFmpeg
This procedure will show you the steps on how to cut videos with FFmpeg

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Editing videos using the Video Processor tool
frontmatter.summary: Steps to process videos with the video processor tool before upload, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Other Process Documents/Editing videos using the Video Processor tool.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Process videos with the video processor tool before upload.
- Outcome: A processed video is exported with the requested volume and trimming settings.
- Trigger: A video needs audio adjustment or start trimming.
- Frequency: As needed before publishing videos.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Source video file and video processor tool.
- Tools: Video processor GUI.
- Inputs: Video file, volume increase value, and cut start duration.

flat_steps.body_md:
First, open the video the needs necessary edits.

    Note: In this case, “LLM Zoomcamp 1.2 - Configuring Your Environment” is the video to be edited.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for first, open the video the needs necessary edits. Look for the red box or arrow around Open, Edit, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After clicking, click on play and identify specific parts of the video that need editing.

    Note: In here, the “ding” sound at the beginning of the video is to be removed.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for after clicking, click on play and identify specific parts of the video that need editing. Look for the red box or arrow around Edit, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After identifying, open the Video Processor tool to make the edits.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 3 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for after identifying, open the Video Processor tool to make the edits. Look for the red box or arrow around Open, Process, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, click on “Browse” to select the file you want to edit.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 4 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for click on "Browse" to select the file you want to edit. Look for the red box or arrow around "Browse", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

This screenshot anchors step 4 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for click on "Browse" to select the file you want to edit. Look for the red box or arrow around "Browse", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Upon clicking the file, the tool processes the video and immediately detects by how much you need to increases the volume of the video.

    Note: In this case, the selected video’s volume needs to be increases by 19.50 dB.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for upon clicking the file, the tool processes the video and immediately detects by how much you need to increases the. Look for the red box or arrow around Process, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, in “Cut Start Duration (seconds)”, input the value in seconds as to where the video should start with consideration to removing the “ding” sound at the beginning of the video.

    Note: If the video has no “ding” sound at the beginning, then, a value of “0” is to be placed.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 6 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for in "Cut Start Duration (seconds)", input the value in seconds as to where the video should start with. Look for the red boxes or arrows around "Cut Start Duration (seconds)", "ding", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Then, check the box beside “Test Run” and click “Process”.

    Note: A Test Run gives a 1 minute video to show the results of the edits made.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 7 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for check the box beside "Test Run" and click "Process". Look for the red boxes or arrows around "Test Run", "Process", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After processing, a dialog box will appear. Click on “Ok” and a new file is created. Select the new file to see the changes made.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 8 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for after processing, a dialog box will appear. Click on "Ok" and a new file is created. Select the new file to see. Look for the red box or arrow around Process, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

This screenshot anchors step 8 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for after processing, a dialog box will appear. Click on "Ok" and a new file is created. Select the new file to see. Look for the red box or arrow around Process, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, go back to the Video Processor tool and uncheck “Test Run” and select “Process” to make the necessary edits on the entire video.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 9 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for go back to the Video Processor tool and uncheck "Test Run" and select "Process" to make the necessary edits on the. Look for the red boxes or arrows around "Test Run", "Process", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After processing, a dialog box showing “Processing completed!” will appear. Click on “Ok”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 10 of the Editing videos using the Video Processor tool process by showing the screen for after processing, a dialog box showing "Processing completed!" will appear. Click on "Ok". Look for the red box or arrow around "Processing completed!", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Increasing Audio in Video using FFmpeg GUI

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Increase video audio volume using the FFmpeg GUI tool.
- Outcome: A processed video with corrected audio is ready for YouTube upload.
- Trigger: A video audio track is too quiet.
- Frequency: As needed before uploading videos.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Local video file and FFmpeg GUI tool.
- Tools: FFmpeg GUI video processor.
- Inputs: Video file and optional volume or cut duration settings.

flat_steps.body_md: The first step is to download the ffmpeg.zip file.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Increasing Audio in Video using FFmpeg GUI process by showing the screen for the first step is to download the ffmpeg.zip file. Look for the red box or arrow around Download, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Open the exe file and double-click it to launch the application.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the Increasing Audio in Video using FFmpeg GUI process by showing the screen for open the exe file and double click it to launch the application. Look for the red box or arrow around Open, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Click “Select Video”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 3 of the Increasing Audio in Video using FFmpeg GUI process by showing the screen for click "Select Video". Look for the red box or arrow around "Select Video", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Videos in the .mov format or extension cannot be visible or accessible in your file browser. To locate such files, type an asterisk or star “\*” in the File name bar and click on"Open ".

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 4 of the Increasing Audio in Video using FFmpeg GUI process by showing the screen for videos in the .mov format or extension cannot be visible or accessible in your file browser. To locate such files. Look for the red box or arrow around "Open", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Click on the video you are supposed to edit.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the Increasing Audio in Video using FFmpeg GUI process by showing the screen for click on the video you are supposed to edit. Look for the red box or arrow around Edit, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: By default, the tool finds the optimum increase in volume. Unless otherwise specified, leave the “Increase Volume By (dB)” field as it automatically detects the appropriate value. Type “0” in the Cut Duration field, then click on “Process Video”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 6 of the Increasing Audio in Video using FFmpeg GUI process by showing the screen for by default, the tool finds the optimum increase in volume. Unless otherwise specified, leave the "Increase Volume. Look for the red boxes or arrows around "Increase Volume By (dB)", "Process Video", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
You will see the video being processed in this window.


    This is the output that you upload to Youtube

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 7 of the Increasing Audio in Video using FFmpeg GUI process by showing the screen for you will see the video being processed in this window. Look for the red box or arrow around Process, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

This screenshot anchors step 7 of the Increasing Audio in Video using FFmpeg GUI process by showing the screen for you will see the video being processed in this window. This is the output that you upload to Youtube. Look for the red box or arrow around Process, Upload, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Updating timecodes for course videos from github issues
frontmatter.summary: Steps to add course video timecodes from GitHub issues to YouTube descriptions, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Other Process Documents/Updating timecodes for course videos from github issues.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Add course video timecodes from GitHub issues to YouTube descriptions.
- Outcome: The YouTube video description contains valid timecodes starting at 00:00.
- Trigger: A GitHub issue or notification provides new video timecodes.
- Frequency: As needed when course video timecodes are submitted.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Course GitHub issue and YouTube Studio.
- Tools: GitHub and YouTube Studio.
- Inputs: GitHub issue with timecodes and the target YouTube video.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open the [Github repo](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/mlops-zoomcamp/issues/13#event-6629270246) or browse any notification from your email when a person adds the timecodes.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Updating timecodes for course videos from github issues process by showing the screen for open the Github repo or browse any notification from your email when a person adds the timecodes. Look for the red box or arrow around Browse, Open, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, click on “view it on Github”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the Updating timecodes for course videos from github issues process by showing the screen for click on "view it on Github". Look for the red box or arrow around "view it on Github", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And then it will redirect you to the Github repo, copy the timecodes and select the YouTube video.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 3 of the Updating timecodes for course videos from github issues process by showing the screen for it will redirect you to the Github repo, copy the timecodes and select the YouTube video. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Once you are in the Youtube video, select “Edit video”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 4 of the Updating timecodes for course videos from github issues process by showing the screen for once you are in the Youtube video, select "Edit video". Look for the red box or arrow around "Edit video", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
And then, paste the timecode of the video.

    Note: If the given timecode doesn’t begin with 00:00, manually add “00:00 \<TITLE\>” in the beginning so that the timecodes will be valid.

    Incorrect time code (doesn’t begin with 00:00)

    00:31 Download and read parquet files.

    01:32 Install missing dependency.

    02:18 Difference between parquet and CSV data

    Correct Timecodes (by adding 00:00 \<TITLE\> in the beginning):

    00:00 Intro and the change in the dataset

    00:31 Download and read parquet files.

    01:32 Install missing dependency.

    02:18 Difference between parquet and CSV data

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the Updating timecodes for course videos from github issues process by showing the screen for , paste the timecode of the video. Incorrect time code (doesn't begin with 00:00) 00:31 Download and read parquet. Look for the red box or arrow around Add, Download, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After updating the timecodes, go back to the GitHub repo and leave a [comment](https://docs.google.com/document/d/15NeUm15z5HPEBKc4dWsFR85lB3thRX1cYWJJL08WGhc/edit?usp=sharing)

    Note: Don’t forget to say “Thank you! I've updated the timecodes” to the person via email.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 7 of the Updating timecodes for course videos from github issues process by showing the screen for if the person removes the timecode in the Github issue, reopen it by clicking "Reopen". Look for the red box or arrow around "Reopen", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Once the person updated the new timecodes, copy the timecodes.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 8 of the Updating timecodes for course videos from github issues process by showing the screen for once the person updated the new timecodes, copy the timecodes. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Email Filtering for Sponsored Workshops
frontmatter.summary: Filter and organize incoming sponsored workshop emails so relevant sponsor and participant messages can be handled efficiently.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Workshops/Email Filtering for Sponsored Workshops_.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose:
- Outcome:
- Trigger:
- Frequency:

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access:
- Tools:
- Inputs:

flat_steps.body_md: On the google sheet, the first thing you need to do is select the “ Would you like to share your email to the sponsor” column and click the filter icon to see those who will be removed in the spreadsheet.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot points to the sponsor email-sharing column in the workshop registration sheet. It helps confirm you are filtering the consent field, not another attendee column.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, uncheck the “Yes” and click “Okay”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the filter choices where “Yes” is removed so only non-consenting attendees remain visible. The “Okay” button applies that temporary view.

flat_steps.body_md: Once done, select the entire sheet with the data of the attendees who said “No” and delete it.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the rows returned by the “No” filter after the consent column is narrowed. Those are the attendee records to remove before sharing data with the sponsor.

flat_steps.body_md: After, go back to the filter icon and check “Yes” and click “Okay”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the filter being switched back to “Yes” after the non-consenting rows are removed. Applying this view leaves only attendees who agreed to share their email.

flat_steps.body_md:
And now, you will be left with attendees who agreed to share their emails to the sponsors.

    Note: In here, those who did not agree to share their emails to the sponsors are removed in the spreadsheet.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the cleaned Google Sheet after the opt-out rows have been deleted. It confirms the remaining attendee list is the sponsor-shareable set.

flat_steps.body_md: To remove the spaces in between the rows, click “Data” and select “Sort Sheet” and click “Sort Sheet by column A (A to Z)”

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Google Sheets Data menu path for sorting the full sheet by column A. Sorting closes gaps left by deleted rows and keeps the sponsor export tidy.

flat_steps.body_md: And here’s the data with all the attendees who agreed to have their emails shared to the sponsors!

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the final sorted attendee data after filtering and cleanup. It is the version that should contain only people who consented to sponsor email sharing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Also, to clean the document before sending it to sponsors, you need to create a new copy of it by clicking “File” and selecting “Make a copy”

    Note: A copy is made to not preserve history because if not, they can undo and see confidential emails.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the File menu option used to make a fresh copy of the spreadsheet. Creating this copy prevents sponsors from accessing prior edit history.

flat_steps.body_md:
Now, you can view the document with no previous version stated.

    Note: Send the copy version to the sponsors

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the copied spreadsheet with no earlier version history visible. This is the sponsor-ready file to share after the consent cleanup.

prose.body_md:
How to Filter Email from Workshops
This document shows the steps on how to Filter Email from Workshops to get the list of emails to share with the sponsors

Step-by-step Instructions

- Inputs:

frontmatter.title: Exporting course registration emails to mailchimp
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for exporting course registration emails and importing them into Mailchimp with the correct course tag.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Mailchimp newsletter/Exporting course registration emails to mailchimp.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Export course registration emails and import them into Mailchimp.
- Outcome: Course registrants are uploaded to Mailchimp with the correct course tag, and the CSV is archived.
- Trigger: New course registration records need to be moved from Airtable to Mailchimp.
- Frequency: Whenever course registration emails are ready to import.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Course Airtable base, Mailchimp audience, and Google Drive mailing list folders.
- Tools: Airtable CSV export, spreadsheet editor, Google Drive, Mailchimp import contacts.
- Inputs: Course registration records, course tag, and date for the exported CSV filename.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you need to do is open [Airtable.com](https://airtable.com/) and select
    - MLOps Zoomcamp for the MLOps Zoomcamp course
    - DE Zoomcamp for the Data Engineering Zoomcamp course
    - ML Zoomcamp for for ML Zoomcamp (Old)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about ML Zoomcamp for for ML Zoomcamp (Old) so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Save all the emails by clicking "Grid view" and choosing "Download CSV"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to save all the emails by clicking "Grid view" and choosing "Download CSV" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Grid view” and “Download CSV”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next, select all the emails by clicking the check box, right-click and choose
    “delete all selected records"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about “delete all selected records" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “delete all selected records”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, select "Delete"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to select "Delete" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Delete”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
To proceed, rename the file name.

    Note: It should this date format: YYYY-MM-DD e.g 2022-05-27

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about it should this date format: YYYY-MM-DD e.g 2022-05-27 so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the schedule or date control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Move the CSV file to [Google drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/19hmJwU06_wYKMgi9RS6GmthBUezurVlR?usp=sharing). Select "Mailing List" then click the folder, "courses"

    Note: For ML Zoomcamp (old), upload the records in this [folder](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Ovzlm3j-8ozJyUWxAw66q0jT1EnxvuXt) and rename the file to dump-YYYY-MM-DD

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to move the CSV file to Google drive. Select "Mailing List" then click the folder, "courses" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Mailing List” and “courses”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, select “[mlops-zoomcamp](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A8TjVwRyXbW7wXyplStM6skaUQrxOxuC?usp=sharing)”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to select “mlops-zoomcamp” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, upload the CSV file, here.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to upload the CSV file, here so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the file transfer or file picker state shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Now, we need to upload the CSV file to Mailchimp.com

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about we need to upload the CSV file to Mailchimp.com so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the file transfer or file picker state shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Once you are in the "Audience dashboard", click "Manage Audience" and select "Import Contacts"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about once you are in the "Audience dashboard", click "Manage Audience" and select "Import Contacts" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Audience dashboard” and “Manage Audience”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Select "Upload a file" then press "Continue"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to select "Upload a file" then press "Continue" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Upload a file” and “Continue”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Click "Browse" and attach the CSV file

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click "Browse" and attach the CSV file so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Browse”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Check the box "Update any existing contacts" and select "Continue to Tag"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to check the box "Update any existing contacts" and select "Continue to Tag" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Update any existing contacts” and “Continue to Tag”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
In the "Search for or create tags" enter the following details
    - *For the MLOps Zoomcamp course, use the tags: "mlops-zoomcamp" and "mlops zoomcamp-1"*

    - *For ML Zoomcamp course, use the tags: “ml-zoomcamp”, “ml-zoomcamp-2”*

    Then click "Continue to Match"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click "Continue to Match" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Continue to Match”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click "Finalize import"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click "Finalize import" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Finalize import”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, select "Complete import"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to select "Complete import" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Complete import”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Once you complete the import, move the CSV file to the "upload" folder.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about once you complete the import, move the CSV file to the "upload" folder so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “upload”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Filling Newsletter Statistics
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for collecting weekly sponsored newsletter performance statistics from Mailchimp, LinkedIn, and X.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Mailchimp newsletter/Filling Newsletter Statistics.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Newsletter Statistics refers to the data concerning the amount of “clicks” and “impressions” of a sponsored link: Mailchimp, LinkedIn and X.
- Outcome: Filling in Newsletter Statistics is important to update our sponsors how well their newsletters perform and to ensure that we track the success of sponsored links accurately.
- Trigger: It is a weekly update (Monday).
- Frequency: Weekly, on Monday.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club schedule spreadsheet, Mailchimp reports, LinkedIn analytics, and X analytics.
- Tools: Google Sheets, Mailchimp, LinkedIn, and X.
- Inputs: Sponsor name, newsletter number, sponsored link, and social media posts.

steps.body_md: Go to DataTalks.Club.schedule [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit?gid=1710801712#gid=1710801712](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit?gid=1710801712#gid=1710801712)

Find in the URL section the newsletter number of the sponsor.

    Note: For this example, the sponsor is Qwak and the newsletter number is #190.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the example shown in the procedure so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Go to [https://mailchimp.com/](https://mailchimp.com/) and click “All campaigns” under it.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to go to https://mailchimp.com/ and click “All campaigns” under it so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “All campaigns”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Scroll through and find the corresponding newsletter of the sponsor guided by the newsletter number.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to scroll through and find the corresponding newsletter of the sponsor guided by the newsletter number so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about once you found the newsletter, click the “View report” button beside it so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “View report”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Copy the number of people who opened the newsletter.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to copy the number of people who opened the newsletter so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to paste it under the “Newsletter impression” column of the sponsor found on the DataTalk.Club.schedule document so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Newsletter impression”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

Go back to the report and scroll down to find the “Click Performance” and copy the amount of clicks of the sponsored link.

    Note: Typically, the sponsored link is the top link.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about typically, the sponsored link is the top link so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Paste it under the “Newsletter clicks” column of the sponsor found on the DataTalk.Club.schedule document.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to paste it under the “Newsletter clicks” column of the sponsor found on the DataTalk.Club.schedule document so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Newsletter clicks”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Go back to the report and click on “Click Performance”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to go back to the report and click on “Click Performance” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Click Performance”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Copy on “Unique clicks”.

frontmatter.title: Getting campaign performance stats
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for gathering sponsor campaign performance metrics across Mailchimp, X, LinkedIn, and the schedule spreadsheet.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Mailchimp newsletter/Getting campaign performance stats.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Collect campaign performance stats for sponsored newsletter and social posts.
- Outcome: Sponsor-facing stats for Mailchimp, Twitter/X, and LinkedIn are gathered.
- Trigger: A sponsor campaign performance report needs to be prepared.
- Frequency: After sponsored campaign activity is available.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Mailchimp reports, DataTalks.Club schedule spreadsheet, X analytics, and LinkedIn analytics.
- Tools: Mailchimp, Google Sheets, X, LinkedIn, and the sponsor stats template.
- Inputs: Sponsor campaign, sponsored links, and related social media announcements.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open MailChimp.com. Under “Campaign” click “All campaign”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open MailChimp.com. Under “Campaign” click “All campaign” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Campaign” and “All campaign”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, find the newsletter and click “View report”

    Note: For easier navigation, you can also utilize the [DataTalks.Club schedule](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit#gid=0) to locate the campaign.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to find the newsletter and click “View report” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “View report”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: To proceed, scroll down and then you can now locate the campaign performance stats.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to scroll down and then you can now locate the campaign performance stats so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the reporting value or action control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: For the email to the sponsor, use this [template](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ipx5NTvNbN3U56HbXQlIynqb9GW7v9NGFgaN6s6_Ia4/edit?usp=sharing) that will serve as your guide in finding the stats.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about for the email to the sponsor, use this template that will serve as your guide in finding the stats so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the email or message detail shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: In finding the total number of clicks on the link, view the section “Top links clicked” and select “View all links”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about in finding the total number of clicks on the link, view the section “Top links clicked” and select “View all links” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Top links clicked” and “View all links”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, find the link to the sponsor with its correspondent click performance

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to find the link to the sponsor with its correspondent click performance so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: To find the views of the newsletter, just hover your mouse towards “Opens” on the newsletter.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about to find the views of the newsletter, just hover your mouse towards “Opens” on the newsletter so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Opens”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: For the performance on Twitter, visit DataTalks.Club’s Twitter and find the sponsored announcement. Click the announcement.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about for the performance on Twitter, visit DataTalks.Club’s Twitter and find the sponsored announcement. Click the announcement so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the post composer or published post shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click “View Twitter Analytics”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click “View Twitter Analytics” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “View Twitter Analytics”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Here, you can view the total views and clicks of the sponsored announcement.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about here, you can view the total views and clicks of the sponsored announcement so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the reporting value or action control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Lastly, to view the total views and clicks on LinkedIn, open DataTalks.Club’s LinkedIn and click on analytics and then “Updates”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about to view the total views and clicks on LinkedIn, open DataTalks.Club’s LinkedIn and click on analytics and then “Updates” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Updates”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Scroll down and find the sponsored announcement and view the total clicks and views of the announcement.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to scroll down and find the sponsored announcement and view the total clicks and views of the announcement so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the reporting value or action control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Once done, update the [spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit#gid=1710801712) for the newsletter performance


    Loom links:

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about update the spreadsheet for the newsletter performance so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Import emails from Luma to Mailchimp
frontmatter.summary: Procedure for importing eligible Luma event guest opt-ins into Mailchimp with the appropriate event tag.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Mailchimp newsletter/Import emails from Luma to Mailchimp.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Import newsletter opt-ins from Luma event guests into Mailchimp.
- Outcome: Eligible Luma guests are added or updated in Mailchimp with the event tag.
- Trigger: A Luma event has guest opt-ins to sync to Mailchimp.
- Frequency: After Luma events when guest opt-ins need to be imported.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Luma event management and Mailchimp audience.
- Tools: Luma guest export, spreadsheet editor, and Mailchimp import contacts.
- Inputs: Luma guest CSV and newsletter opt-in responses.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is visit “[https://lu.ma/home](https://lu.ma/home)”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about visit “https://lu.ma/home” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “[https://lu.ma/home](https://lu.ma/home)”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click on "Events” and the “Past” button.

This procedure will show you the steps on how to create a document for sponsored content for a newsletter.

steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open the [Newsletter](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1QmU2KvUOHWwJ6njLJpag94STRstrlUdu) folder on google drive.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open the Newsletter folder on google drive so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the folder or Drive location shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md:
Then, rename the title of the newsletter.
    It should follow this format: “YYYY-MM-DD - Company name” (No need to to add “TBA”)

    We have 3 different types of Newsletter:
Main Slot - YYYY-MM-DD - Company name
Secondary Slot - YYYY-MM-DD - Company name (Secondary Slot)
Stand-alone Newsletter - YYYY-MM-DD - Company name (Stand-alone newsletter)

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Copy the sponsor copy, visual, and CTA link into the newsletter sponsored block.
- Outcome: The sponsored block has the sponsor image, CTA link, title, and text in Mailchimp.
- Trigger: The sponsor content is ready and approved by Valeria or Alexey.
- Frequency: For each sponsored newsletter placement.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Mailchimp newsletter draft and the sponsorship document.
- Tools: Mailchimp campaign editor, sponsor document, image download tool.
- Inputs: Sponsor visual, CTA link, title, and body text.

What: copying the copy, the visual and the CTA (call-to-action) link from the sponsorship document to the sponsored section of the newsletter

Why: the sponsors share their content with us via the sponsorship document and we need to transfer it to mailchimp’s campaign editor

When: the sponsor is ready with their content and our content team (Valeria or Alexey) checked it and said it’s okay

steps.body_md: Open the newsletter draft.

groups.title: Updating the visual for the sponsored slot

steps.body_md:
Download the visual (the image) that the sponsor sent us.

    If they included the image in the document, click the picture and select “View more actions” and click “Save to Keep”

    Then, find the image on the Keep Menu and click it then save the image.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about if they included the image in the document, click the picture and select “View more actions” and click “Save to Keep” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “View more actions” and “Save to Keep”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

This screenshot anchors the step to find the image on the Keep Menu and click it then save the image so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the reporting value or action control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Then click on the sponsored block title and select “Upload Image”

steps.body_md: After, click “Upload”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click “Upload” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Upload”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Then select the picture you want to upload.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to select the picture you want to upload so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the file transfer or file picker state shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Once the event banner is added, proceed to copy the link of the sponsor from the sponsorship document.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about once the event banner is added, proceed to copy the link of the sponsor from the sponsorship document so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Then go back to Luma, click “Link” on the image.

Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Adding Contacts on the CRM Table
frontmatter.summary: Explains how to add sponsor contact records to the CRM with company links and lead status details.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Sales/CRM/Adding Contacts on the CRM Table_.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Add sponsor contacts to the CRM contacts table.
- Outcome: The contact record is created with relevant company and lead status details.
- Trigger: A new sponsor contact needs to be tracked.
- Frequency: As needed

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Airtable CRM and contact source information.
- Tools: Airtable, LinkedIn.
- Inputs: Contact name, company, role, LinkedIn profile, email if available, and lead status.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is click “Contacts” on Airtable.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around "Contacts", then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

flat_steps.body_md:
And then, click the expand icon to add contact and information.

    Note: You can also add the contact and information by typing on the empty space.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around the highlighted table, record, field, status, or linked value, then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

flat_steps.body_md:
Now, enter the name of the person; his/her role in the company; date of the last contact company name; email (if given); LinkedIn account; the lead status; and some notes or comment you want to add

    Note: Here are meaning of the lead statuses:

    Prospect - sent connection request on LinkedIn

    Cold - contacted them, it was a cold outreach, nobody introduced us to them

    Hot - Somebody introduce as to them or Alexey have met somebody and contact was established

    Proposed - When we talked to them and had a conversation or discussion and sent them an offer

    Deal - Clients that agreed to the proposal

    Introduced collegue - We reached not to the right people (e.g. Account Executive). People who don't own the budget of the marketing or partnerships but he/she can introduce us to his/her colleague who are in marketing or partnerships.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around the highlighted table, record, field, status, or linked value, then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around the highlighted table, record, field, status, or linked value, then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

After, add the name/description of the deal.

    Note: Deals can be a newsletter promotion, workshop, and sponsorship. In this example, the deal is a newsletter promotion

flat_steps.body_md: Next, add the company and the contact person of the company.

flat_steps.body_md:

summary.body_md:
