- Trigger: When you get a TODO about some promoted content from a newsletter, social media, etc
- Frequency: As needed

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Airtable sponsorship CRM and source content.
- Tools: Airtable, LinkedIn or source platform.
- Inputs: Content link, sponsor/company name, content type, source, and company details.

steps.body_md: Open the CRM database in Airtable [https://airtable.com/](https://airtable.com/)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around the highlighted table, record, field, status, or linked value, then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

steps.body_md:
Select the “Companies” section and search if there is already an existing record of the company.

    Note: If there is no existing record proceed to the next step

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around "Companies", then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

steps.body_md: Look for the “Add” button on the lower left side of the screen and click to add.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around "Add", then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

steps.body_md: A new record would be created and select the “Expand” button on the side. A tab would appear where you are tasked to provide information, add the name and the link of the sponsored content on the corresponding lines.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around "Expand", then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

steps.body_md:
After, add the content type and the source of the sponsored content

    Note: SInce this is an ebook, the content type is ebook and the source on where you can view it, is on LinkedIn. For Organic contents, these are content that they are promoting through newsletter, LinkedIn post, and other social media platforms.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around the highlighted table, record, field, status, or linked value, then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

steps.body_md:
Then, add the Relevancy and the company who sponsored the content on LinkedIn.

    Note: Don’t forget to add the sponsor’s website and other information

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around the highlighted table, record, field, status, or linked value, then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around the highlighted table, record, field, status, or linked value, then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

steps.body_md: After saving the company, navigate back to home and click the “Content” section in the CRM.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around "Content", then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

steps.body_md: Click on “Add Record” on the bottom of the screen and expand to create a new content record.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around "Add Record", then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

steps.body_md: Expand the record fields and input the following information. Add the direct link to the content, add the Content Type, choose the relevant type. For a webinar, select “Event.” Select the content source. For example, “Newsletter” if it was shared via a newsletter.For “Comment”, enter a brief description, such as the name of the newsletter source.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the CRM update in Airtable CRM. Look for the red callout around "Comment", then update the record so the CRM data stays consistent.

steps.body_md:
Click “Save” and verify that the content record is added under the company.


     Loom links:

frontmatter.title: Creating UTM Tags for Email Campaigns
frontmatter.summary: Explains how to generate UTM-tagged sponsor URLs for email, newsletter, and social campaign tracking.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Other Process Documents/Creating UTM Tags for Email Campaigns.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Create tracked URLs for sponsored email and social campaigns.
- Outcome: A UTM-tagged sponsor URL is generated and added to the sponsored document.
- Trigger: A sponsor link needs tracking for a campaign.
- Frequency: For each sponsored campaign link that needs UTM tracking.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Sponsor document and URL builder.
- Tools: UTM builder and sponsor document.
- Inputs: Sponsor URL, campaign source, medium, and campaign name.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is visit, [https://web.utm.io/utm-builder-code-generator/](https://web.utm.io/utm-builder-code-generator/)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the Creating UTM Tags for Email Campaigns process by showing the screen for visit, https://web.utm.io/utm builder code generator/. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
And then, add the website URL given by the sponsor and the campaign source.

    Note: In here, since the source is newsletter campaign, add “newsletter” as the campaign source. If it’s LinkedIn or Twitter, it’s “Social”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the Creating UTM Tags for Email Campaigns process by showing the screen for , add the website URL given by the sponsor and the campaign source. Look for the red box or arrow around Add, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Then, add the campaign medium which is “DTC” and the campaign name as “DTC-\<YYYY\>-\<MM\>-\<DD\>

    Note: If the campaign is through Social Media, follow this format: “DTC-\<Name of Social Media”\> e.g. DTC-twitter

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 3 of the Creating UTM Tags for Email Campaigns process by showing the screen for add the campaign medium which is "DTC" and the campaign name as "DTC \<YYYY\ \<MM\ \<DD\. Look for the red box or arrow around Add, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: To generate the UTW, click “Build URL”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 4 of the Creating UTM Tags for Email Campaigns process by showing the screen for to generate the UTW, click "Build URL". Look for the red box or arrow around "Build URL", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Lastly, copy the URL and paste it on the sponsored document.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the Creating UTM Tags for Email Campaigns process by showing the screen for copy the URL and paste it on the sponsored document. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

frontmatter.title: How to use Figma for creating event banners
frontmatter.summary: Explains how to create event banners in Figma using DataTalks.Club templates, speaker images, titles, and logos.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Other Process Documents/How to use Figma for creating event banners_.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Create event banners from DataTalks.Club Figma templates.
- Outcome: Event banner assets are updated with the event title, guest, image, and logos.
- Trigger: A new event needs banner images.
- Frequency: For each event that needs promotional banners.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club event banners file in Figma.
- Tools: Figma and browser-based image sources.
- Inputs: Event title, event type, speaker name, speaker image, and sponsor logos when applicable.

flat_steps.body_md:
The first thing you need to do is open "[figma.com](http://figma.com)"

    Note: Event banners are ready to be used. You only need to edit the name of the event, picture, and name of the guest; and other necessary edits (e.g. color of the text, size, etc...)

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 1 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for open "figma.com". Look for the red box or arrow around "figma.com", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: On the left side, select “Templates.”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 2 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for on the left side, select "Templates.". Look for the red box or arrow around "Templates", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Choose the specific template in accordance to the speaker’s event type.

    Note: Each template is captioned with the type of event, for how many speakers, and for sponsored events. Ex: Podcast-1 speaker. The banner used for Luma is the square shaped template and the banner used for Youtube is the rectangular shaped template.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 3 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for choose the specific template in accordance to the speaker's event type. Look for the red box, arrow, selected row, or highlighted screen area, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next, zoom in to the specific template and start to edit the title of the event.

    Note: In this example, the title of the event is: "Load Testing ML Microservices for robustness and scalability". In addition, make sure to align the logos in the center, especially for sponsored event banners.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 4 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for zoom in to the specific template and start to edit the title of the event. Look for the red box or arrow around Next, Edit, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, add the name of the guest.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 5 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for add the name of the guest. Look for the red box or arrow around Add, then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: To change the picture of the guest, click on the photo, and on the left side of your screen, click on "Person_1"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 6 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for to change the picture of the guest, click on the photo, and on the left side of your screen, click on "Person 1". Look for the red box or arrow around "Person 1", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After clicking, select "Photo" and click "CTRL + V HERE"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 7 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for after clicking, select "Photo" and click "CTRL + V HERE". Look for the red boxes or arrows around "Photo", "CTRL + V HERE", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: On the right side of your screen, you can see a small image. Click on that small image icon and select "Choose Image"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 8 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for on the right side of your screen, you can see a small image. Click on that small image icon and select "Choose. Look for the red box or arrow around "Choose Image", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: From here, choose the image from your computer and select "Open"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 9 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for from here, choose the image from your computer and select "Open". Look for the red box or arrow around "Open", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After uploading the picture, click "Export Event_banner" on the left side of your screen.

    Note: Make sure to double-check the grammar of the cover using Grammarly.com

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors step 10 of the How to use Figma for creating event banners process by showing the screen for after uploading the picture, click "Export Event banner" on the left side of your screen. Look for the red box or arrow around "Export Event banner", then use that highlighted area as the target for the action before continuing.

prose.body_md:

frontmatter.title: Creating Sponsored Content for LinkedIn Post
frontmatter.summary: Steps to create a sponsored LinkedIn post from newsletter sponsor materials, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Social Media/LinkedIn/Creating Sponsored Content for LinkedIn Post.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Create a sponsored LinkedIn post from newsletter sponsor materials.
- Outcome: A LinkedIn sponsored post is drafted or scheduled with text, link, and image.
- Trigger: Sponsored newsletter promotional content needs a LinkedIn post.
- Frequency: For each sponsored LinkedIn promotion.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club LinkedIn page and Trello sponsorship card.
- Tools: LinkedIn and Trello.
- Inputs: Sponsorship document, LinkedIn post template, registration link, and banner image.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open [https://www.linkedin.com/company/datatalks-club/](https://www.linkedin.com/company/datatalks-club/) and click the “Create” button.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open https://www.linkedin.com/company/datatalks-club/ and click the “Create” button so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Create”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, click “Start a post”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click “Start a post” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Start a post”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
In another tab, go to Trello and select the card that contains the sponsored newsletter. Open the sponsorship document to look for templates.

    Note: For this example, the sponsor is DoubleCloud and we will follow the template on the corresponding sponsorship document provided.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the example shown in the procedure so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Copy the template under “Webinar” for LinkedIn posts.

    Note: Only copy the text. Do not copy embedded files like “webinar banners_1200x628.png”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about only copy the text. Do not copy embedded files like “webinar banners_1200x628.png” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “webinar banners_1200x628.png”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Paste the text on the draft post you started on LinkedIn. Make sure to mention the sponsor’s account in the post.

    Note: You can mention accounts by putting “@” before the name. Different accounts might appear, so make sure to click the correct one.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about you can mention accounts by putting “@” before the name. Different accounts might appear, so make sure to click the correc... so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the reporting value or action control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: In some cases, you might need to edit the texts. Make sure to add bullet points for the outline. Preferably, a diamond bullet.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about in some cases, you might need to edit the texts. Make sure to add bullet points for the outline. Preferably, a diamond bullet so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Go back to the sponsorship document and copy the registration link.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to go back to the sponsorship document and copy the registration link so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Put “Register now:” at the end of the post and paste the link after the colon.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about put “Register now:” at the end of the post and paste the link after the colon so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Register now:”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Remove the preview that will appear after the link by clicking the “x” button.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to remove the preview that will appear after the link by clicking the “x” button so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “x”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Go back to the sponsorship document. Click the edited webinar banner file and download it.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to go back to the sponsorship document. Click the edited webinar banner file and download it so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the file transfer or file picker state shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Click the “*add media*” button and upload the edited webinar banner from your files.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click the “*add media*” button and upload the edited webinar banner from your files so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “*add media*”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After the “Register now: \[LINK\]” text, *leave* three (3) spaces or breaks and indicate that the LinkedIn post is sponsored through this format:

    “This post is sponsored by \[SPONSOR\]. Thank you for supporting our community!”

    Note: The sponsor’s account should also be mentioned.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about “This post is sponsored by [SPONSOR]. Thank you for supporting our community!” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the post composer or published post shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Now that the edited banner has been placed, click the “*schedule for later”* button to schedule the post.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about that the edited banner has been placed, click the “*schedule for later”* button to schedule the post so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “*schedule for later”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Edit the date and time according to the corresponding content plan. Typically, the scheduled post for sponsored content is on a *Wednesday*.

    Note: LinkedIn does not let you define the timezone. So, make sure that the time is aligned to the Central European Time (CET).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about linkedIn does not let you define the timezone. So, make sure that the time is aligned to the Central European Time (CET) so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the schedule or date control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
To help you with the timezone, you can search for conversion from your timezone to CET or Berlin timezone on google.

    Note: In this example, we are converting 9:00 AM Berlin Time to Manila Time. It is shown that 9:00 AM in Berlin is 4:00 PM in Manila. So, if you are trying to schedule a 9:00 AM CET time (but you are in a Manila Timezone) , schedule it at 4:00 PM.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the example shown in the procedure so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the schedule or date control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After that, click the “*schedule*” button to schedule the post.


    After the post has been posted.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click the “*schedule*” button to schedule the post so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “*schedule*”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Copy the link to the sponsored LinkedIn post.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to copy the link to the sponsored LinkedIn post so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Open Trello and paste the link of the LinkedIn post on the corresponding sponsored newsletter card.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open Trello and paste the link of the LinkedIn post on the corresponding sponsored newsletter card so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Open the DataTalks.Club schedule sheets [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit?gid=1710801712#gid=1710801712](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit?gid=1710801712#gid=1710801712) and paste the link of the LinkedIn post under the “*LinkedIn post”* column of the corresponding sponsor.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open the DataTalks.Club schedule sheets https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2... so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “*LinkedIn post”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Log-in to LinkedIn using Alexey’s account. Click the "Like" button of the sponsored post and comment depending on the content. This is to amplify the post, so that many people would see it.

    Note: In this example, the comment is “Looking forward to it!”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the example shown in the procedure so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Looking forward to it!”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

prose.body_md:
Creating Sponsored Content for LinkedIn Post
This procedure will show you the steps on how to schedule posts with LinkedIn and post about newsletter promotional content.

Step-by-step instructions

frontmatter.title: Make an announcement on LinkedIn
frontmatter.summary: Steps to publish a DataTalks.Club announcement on LinkedIn, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Social Media/LinkedIn/Make an announcement on LinkedIn.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Publish a DataTalks.Club announcement on LinkedIn.
- Outcome: The announcement is posted from the DataTalks.Club LinkedIn page with a follow-up comment.
- Trigger: A podcast or event announcement is ready to publish.
- Frequency: As needed for announcements.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club LinkedIn page and announcement template.
- Tools: LinkedIn and Notion template.
- Inputs: Announcement text and speaker or podcast details.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open the podcast announcement template on Notion.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open the podcast announcement template on Notion so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After, visit LinkedIn and select "Start a post”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about visit LinkedIn and select "Start a post” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Start a post”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, select the button- right beside the profile picture

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to select the button- right beside the profile picture so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the file transfer or file picker state shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And select “DataTalks.Club”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about and select “DataTalks.Club” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “DataTalks.Club”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After, paste the podcast announcement on the space provided and click “Post”

    Note: Don’t include dots in LinkedIn announcement

    Wrong: “..DataTalks.Club”

    Correct:”...DataTalksClub”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about correct:”...DataTalksClub” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
After posting, open the message as Alexey’s account and write in the comments: “Cool stuff. Thanks \<name\> for coming to the podcast!”

    Possible comments:

    - “Cool stuff”
    - “Looking forward to the talk \<NAME OF THE SPEAKER\>”
    - ‘Looking forward to it!’
    - “Looking forward to the chat \<NAME OF THE SPEAKER\>”
    - “See you next week, \<NAME OF THE SPEAKER\>”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about “See you next week, NAME OF THE SPEAKER” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “See you next week, \<NAME OF THE SPEAKER\>”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

prose.body_md:
How to make an announcement on LinkedIn
This procedure will show you the steps on how to make an announcement on LinkedIn.

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.title: Reach out to people on LinkedIn + following a recommendation
frontmatter.summary: Steps to contact recommended prospective guests on LinkedIn, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Social Media/LinkedIn/Reach out to people on LinkedIn + following a recommendation.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Contact recommended prospective guests on LinkedIn.
- Outcome: A connection request with a short invitation note is sent.
- Trigger: Someone recommends a potential podcast or event guest.
- Frequency: As needed during guest outreach.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: LinkedIn account used for outreach.
- Tools: LinkedIn.
- Inputs: Recommended person, recommender name when available, and outreach note.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is to reach out to the person recommended, through LinkedIn.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about to reach out to the person recommended, through LinkedIn so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Once you are in their LinkedIn profile, click "Connect" and select "Add a note"

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about once you are in their LinkedIn profile, click "Connect" and select "Add a note" so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Connect” and “Add a note”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
And now, you can enter a note or message for the person and click "Send".
    Example note:

    “Hi Remi! I'm \>NAME\<, Community Manager at DataTalks.Club. We organize live podcasts that feature guests from the data field. Would you be interested in being a guest?”

    Note: Don't forget to include the name of the person who recommended them, if any. In this example, it was Noa Tamir who recommended Stefan.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about don't forget to include the name of the person who recommended them, if any. summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Outcome: The LinkedIn post is scheduled with copy, media, and sponsor disclosure.
- Trigger: Newsletter promotional content needs to be scheduled on LinkedIn.
- Frequency: As needed for sponsored newsletter promotions.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Hootsuite and the DataTalks.Club LinkedIn account.
- Tools: Hootsuite Publisher.
- Inputs: Post copy, schedule date/time, media file, and sponsor disclosure.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open [Hootsuite](https://hootsuite.com/dashboard#/home) and on the right side of your screen, click the calendar icon, “Publisher”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open Hootsuite and on the right side of your screen, click the calendar icon, “Publisher” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Publisher”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
In the calendar, select the time and date you wish to schedule your post and select “Post”

    Note: For LinkedIn posts, it is usually scheduled every 8 AM CET.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about for LinkedIn posts, it is usually scheduled every 8 AM CET so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the schedule or date control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
And then, select what social account you will be using.

    Note: In this example, we will be posting through our LinkedIn account.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the example shown in the procedure so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And now, enter the description of the newsletter promotional content under “Content”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about and now, enter the description of the newsletter promotional content under “Content” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Content”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Don’t forget to add: “This post is sponsored by \<Company\>. Thank you for your support!”

    Note: Separate it with three line breaks at least from the last sentence or phrase e.g “Register here: xxx”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about separate it with three line breaks at least from the last sentence or phrase e.g “Register here: xxx” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Register here: xxx”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: To upload a picture, click “Drag files here or select files to upload” under “Media”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about to upload a picture, click “Drag files here or select files to upload” under “Media” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Drag files here or select files to upload” and “Media”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Lastly, select “Schedule”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to select “Schedule” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Schedule”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Creating a Visual for a Book Giveaway Campaign on Twitter

- Outcome: Book giveaway images are updated and ready to post.
- Trigger: A book giveaway campaign needs promotional visuals.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Previous giveaway slide/template and book cover source.
- Tools: Google Slides or equivalent screenshot tool.
- Inputs: Book title, book cover image URL, giveaway details, and previous visual template.

flat_steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is duplicate the slide from the previous book giveaway campaign.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about duplicate the slide from the previous book giveaway campaign so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After duplicating, edit the information/text in the slide.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about duplicating, edit the information/text in the slide so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Next, copy the image address of the book. Right-click and select “Copy Image Address”

    Note: You can visit the publisher’s page to view the book cover.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to copy the image address of the book. Right-click and select “Copy Image Address” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Copy Image Address”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Go back to the Google slide, right-click the picture and point your mouse to “Replace Image” and select “By URL”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to go back to the Google slide, right-click the picture and point your mouse to “Replace Image” and select “By URL” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Replace Image” and “By URL”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: And then, paste the image address under the “Replace Image” field.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to paste the image address under the “Replace Image” field so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Replace Image”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, click “Replace”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click “Replace” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Replace”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
Once done, repeat the process to the other images.

    Note: In this example, we repeated the process 5 times since there are 5 images.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the example shown in the procedure so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the schedule or date control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
To proceed, take a screenshot of the Visual.

    Note: You can use any software to take screenshots or use “Window + Shift +S”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about you can use any software to take screenshots or use “Window + Shift +S” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Window + Shift +S”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: In announcing the giveaway, open DataTalks.Club’s Twitter account and select “Tweet”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about in announcing the giveaway, open DataTalks.Club’s Twitter account and select “Tweet” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Tweet”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, paste the copied image on the clipboard, and press “Ctrl + V”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to paste the copied image on the clipboard, and press “Ctrl + V” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Ctrl + V”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md:
And enter the caption of the book giveaway.

    Note: Tag the author’s Twitter account and include the event page link in the caption.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about tagging the author’s Twitter account and including the event page link in the caption so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

flat_steps.body_md: After reviewing the caption, click “Tweet”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about reviewing the caption, click “Tweet” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Tweet”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Creating post for sponsored content on X
frontmatter.summary: Steps to create and schedule a sponsored content post on X, with guidance for the related DataTalks.Club workflow.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Social Media/Twitter/Creating post for sponsored content on X_.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Create and schedule a sponsored content post on X.
- Outcome: The sponsored X post is scheduled with copy, link, banner, and timing.
- Trigger: Sponsored newsletter content needs an X promotion.
- Frequency: For each sponsored X promotion.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: DataTalks.Club X account and Trello sponsorship card.
- Tools: X and Trello.
- Inputs: Sponsorship document, Twitter/X template, registration link, banner image, and schedule time.

steps.body_md: The first thing you need to do is open [https://x.com/DataTalksClub](https://x.com/DataTalksClub) and click the “Post” button.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open https://x.com/DataTalksClub and click the “Post” button so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Post”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: On another tab, open Trello and click the sponsorship document found in the corresponding sponsored newsletter card to find templates.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about on another tab, open Trello and click the sponsorship document found in the corresponding sponsored newsletter card to fin... so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the reporting value or action control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: In the sponsorship document, copy the text under the “Template for Twitter.”

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about in the sponsorship document, copy the text under the “Template for Twitter.” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Template for Twitter.”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md:
Paste it on the draft post on X.

    Note: X has a text limit. With this, we need to erase some descriptions and make the text shorter so that it will not exceed the limit. Don’t forget to follow [proper punctuation](https://docs.google.com/document/d/192lEpUc6WemtooqcqNiHub-7OTTJAHe2k-mJg-bUWYI/edit?usp=sharing) and spacing.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to paste it on the draft post on X so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Remove the preview that will appear after the link.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to remove the preview that will appear after the link so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Go back to the sponsorship document. Find the edited webinar banner file and download it.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to go back to the sponsorship document. Find the edited webinar banner file and download it so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the file transfer or file picker state shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Click the “media” button and upload the edited webinar banner from your files.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click the “media” button and upload the edited webinar banner from your files so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “media”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: After the edited webinar banner has been added, click the “schedule” button to schedule the post.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about the edited webinar banner has been added, click the “schedule” button to schedule the post so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “schedule”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md:
Edit the date and time according to the corresponding content plan. Typically, the scheduled post for sponsored content is on a *Wednesday*.

    Note: X does not let you select a timezone. So, make sure that the time is aligned to Central European Timezone (CET) or Berlin Time.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to edit the date and time according to the corresponding content plan. Typically, the scheduled post for sponsored content is... so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the schedule or date control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md:
To help you with the timezone, you can search for conversion from your timezone to CET or Berlin timezone on google.

    Note: In this example, we are converting 9:00 AM Berlin Time to Manila Time. It is shown that 9:00 AM in Berlin is 4:00 PM in Manila. So, if you are trying to schedule a 9:00 AM CET time (but you are in a Manila Timezone) , schedule it at 4:00 PM.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the example shown in the procedure so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the schedule or date control shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: After that, click the “schedule” button to schedule the post.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to click the “schedule” button to schedule the post so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “schedule”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

groups.title: After the post has been posted.

steps.body_md:
Comment on the post and indicate that it is sponsored through this format:

    “This post is sponsored by \[SPONSOR\]. Thank you for supporting our community!”

    Note: The account of the sponsor should be mentioned.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about “This post is sponsored by [SPONSOR]. Thank you for supporting our community!” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the post composer or published post shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Copy the link to the sponsored X post.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to copy the link to the sponsored X post so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Open Trello and paste the link of the X post on the corresponding sponsored newsletter card. Save the changes.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open Trello and paste the link of the X post on the corresponding sponsored newsletter card. Save the changes so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the link, copy, or paste target shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Open the DataTalks.Club schedule sheets [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit?gid=1710801712#gid=1710801712](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2s/edit?gid=1710801712#gid=1710801712) and paste the link of the X post under the “*Twitter post”* column of the corresponding sponsor.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open the DataTalks.Club schedule sheets https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-T8qkmShlFUrT2NmkI8Pi1NgUS9IunP6wO5-L79xe2... so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “*Twitter post”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Log-in on X using Alexey’s account. Click the "Like" and “Repost” button of the sponsored post. This is to amplify the post, so that many people would see it.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about log-in on X using Alexey’s account. Click the "Like" and “Repost” button of the sponsored post. This is to amplify the pos... so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Like” and “Repost”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

frontmatter.title: Saving voice notes from Telegram
frontmatter.summary: Steps to save Telegram voice notes and transcribe them with Groq Console for later task reference.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Social Media/Twitter/Saving voice notes from Telegram.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: To save voice notes from Telegram and use the Groq Console for transcribing.
- Outcome: It helps keep voice messages organized so they can be easily used as a reference later.
- Trigger: When there is a new voice message that can be used for a task.
- Frequency: As needed when useful voice notes are received.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Telegram chat and Groq Console.
- Tools: Telegram desktop and Groq Console Whisper transcription.
- Inputs: Telegram voice note audio file.

steps.body_md: Open the Telegram app on your computer and go to the DTC Chat.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open the Telegram app on your computer and go to the DTC Chat so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md:
Scroll up to the voice note you need to save and click the download button.

    Note: The download button will look like a downward ↓ arrow. Once downloaded, it turns into a “play” button as shown below.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about the download button will look like a downward ↓ arrow. Once downloaded, it turns into a “play” button as shown below so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “play”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Once downloaded, right-click the voice note to open a pop-up menu. On this menu, click “Save as…”.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step about once downloaded, right-click the voice note to open a pop-up menu. On this menu, click “Save as…” so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Save as…”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Choose where to save the voice note in your folder.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to choose where to save the voice note in your folder so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the folder or Drive location shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md: Open the [Groq](https://console.groq.com/playground) application on your browser then click “llama3-8b-8192” on the upper right corner of the screen to reveal a drop-down list. Select “whisper-large-v3” on this list.

groups.title: Summarizing tasks from the voice note with ChatGPT

steps.body_md: Open [ChatGPT](http://chatgpt.com).

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to open ChatGPT so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for the relevant screen area shown there, then use it to confirm you are in the correct place before continuing.

steps.body_md:
Now, copy and paste the text below into the chat box on ChatGPT. This is the prompt for ChatGPT.

    “Based on the provided transcript of the voice note, give me the summary of this voice note and all the actions that I need to perform based on the transcript of the voice note.

    Transcript:”

    Note: Remove quotation marks (“).

Paste the text you copied under the word... so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “Transcript:” and “Enter”, then use those cues to complete or verify the step before continuing.

steps.body_md: Scroll down to the end of the summary and click the “copy” icon on the lower left to copy the summary.

screenshots.caption: This screenshot anchors the step to scroll down to the end of the summary and click the “copy” icon on the lower left to copy the summary so you can match the documented UI before acting. Look for “copy”, then use that cue to complete or verify the step before continuing.

## Summary

- Outcome: The promotional tweet is scheduled and sponsor disclosure is added after posting.
- Trigger: Newsletter promotional content needs to be posted on Twitter/X.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites

flat_steps.body_md:
And then, enter the title and the description of the newsletter promotional content.

    Note: Twitter has a text limit. With this, we need to erase some descriptions and make the text shorter so that it will not exceed the limit. Don’t forget to follow [proper punctuation](https://docs.google.com/document/d/192lEpUc6WemtooqcqNiHub-7OTTJAHe2k-mJg-bUWYI/edit?usp=sharing) and spacing.

flat_steps.body_md:
To proceed, use emojis to save space.

    Note: Inside the box is an example of the shorter version of the description from Step 2. Make sure to the link is always the same thing as the newsletter. Moreover, double-check the* [*extra spaces*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mTxJE0kjNKObpAHBx9KsLAqOdMEMYPXuB5cBFMd9ce4/edit?usp=sharing) *in the text. Also, don’t include dots in the text.

Make sure to the link is always the sa... so you can match the documented UI before acting. frontmatter.title: Creating Dashboards for Airtable
frontmatter.summary: Explains how to create Airtable dashboards that show course registration counts and simple interface summaries.
frontmatter.source: Processes/Airtable/Creating Dashboards for Airtable.docx

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Purpose: Create Airtable dashboards for course registration tables.
- Outcome: An Airtable interface shows the registration count for a course.
- Trigger: A course base needs a simple registration dashboard.
- Frequency: Once per course base.

prerequisites.body_md:
## Prerequisites


- Access: Airtable course base.
- Tools: Airtable Interfaces.
- Inputs: Course registration table and dashboard name.

flat_steps.body_md:
First, go to the database you are interested in.

    Note: In this process, we will be creating a “MLOps Zoomcamp” dashboard.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the selected Airtable base for the course dashboard. It helps confirm you are starting from the correct course database before opening interface tools.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, go to “Interfaces”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Interfaces entry in Airtable. This is the workspace area used to build dashboards on top of table data.

flat_steps.body_md: After which, click on “Start Building”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Start Building button for a new Airtable interface. It marks the point where dashboard setup begins.

flat_steps.body_md: After, click on “Build an Interface”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Build an Interface option. Choosing it starts the interface builder rather than a different Airtable view.

flat_steps.body_md: Upon clicking, name the interface according to preference.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the interface naming step. Use this field to give the dashboard a recognizable course or cohort name.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, click on “Dashboard”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Dashboard layout option in Airtable Interfaces. Selecting it creates a dashboard-style interface for registration metrics.

flat_steps.body_md: To proceed, click on “Next”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Next button after choosing the dashboard layout. This advances to the table selection step.

flat_steps.body_md: On the Table options, select a Table name and click “Finish”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the table picker and Finish button. It confirms which Airtable table will supply data to the dashboard.

flat_steps.body_md: Then, customize the Dashboard.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the dashboard editor with configurable elements. This is where the dashboard can be adjusted before it is shared.

flat_steps.body_md: After customizing, click on “Publish”.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the Publish control for the Airtable interface. Publishing makes the customized dashboard available to viewers.

flat_steps.body_md: To share, fill in the necessary information.

screenshots.caption: The screenshot shows the sharing dialog for the published dashboard. It indicates where to enter access details before sending or saving the share settings.

prose.body_md:
How to Create Dashboards for Airtable
This document shows the steps on How to Create Dashboards for Airtable.

Step-by-step Instructions

frontmatter.summary: Git-backed DataTasks template for the Open-Source Spotlight operational workflow.
frontmatter.source: backend/scripts/seed-templates.ts

summary.body_md:
## Summary

- Template type: `oss`
- Trigger: manual
- Task count: 14
- Anchor date: planned YouTube publication date
- Anchor time policy: schedule for Wednesday 17:00 Europe/Berlin unless the operator records a source-backed exception.
- Default assignee ID: `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001`

purpose.body_md:
## Purpose

Run one pre-recorded Open-Source Spotlight video from project selection and author outreach through YouTube publication, playlist confirmation, guest follow-up, and social announcement proof.

This is operator work, not a documentation index. Each task carries due offsets from the publication date, phase/stage context, required proof, waiting/follow-up guidance, and a stable instruction document ID when an in-repo process document exists.

references.body_md:
## References

- [Process documents](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1FEmQV8myR3jN-8_kCG_tQh4jrrxFZJPpRag9iPf_RII/edit)
- [Events](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1SVWxBsBzvG5URX2tWD9M9HRfI11c2eq3Z7TMt0-JHqQ/edit)
- [Events (pre-recorded) - Open-Source Spotlight](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1foX7pya-Ywi153LkZWFWBw2nI6HYvcQKS-QQBEUmGZc/edit)

Stable source document IDs:

- `task-template.tasks.oss`
- `reference.overview.events`
- `reference.overview.events-pre-recorded-open-source-spotlight`
- `sop.media.open-source-spotlight.reach-out-to-open-source-spotlight-guests`
- `sop.media.open-source-spotlight.joining-open-source-project-communities-and-asking-for-oss-demos`
- `sop.media.open-source-spotlight.filling-in-the-open-source-spotlight-airtable-database`
- `sop.media.open-source-spotlight.find-timestamps-for-editing`
- `sop.media.open-source-spotlight.adding-links-from-the-zoom-chat`
- `sop.media.open-source-spotlight.adding-timecodes-for-open-source-spotlight-videos`
- `sop.media.open-source-spotlight.schedule-open-source-spotlight-youtube-videos`
- `template.media.open-source-spotlight.oss-reaching-out-to-authors-about-their-tool`
- `template.media.open-source-spotlight.oss-asking-for-revisions-and-links`
- `template.media.open-source-spotlight.oss-ask-the-guests-to-share-the-videos-with-their-networks`
- `reference.media.open-source-spotlight.download-open-source-spotlight-video-from-zoom-and-upload-it-to-youtube`
- `reference.social-media.post-oss`
- `sop.media.video-youtube.add-timecodes-to-youtube-videos`
- `sop.media.video-youtube.adding-videos-from-other-channels-to-our-playlist`

phases.body_md:
## Phases And Stages

- `lead-outreach`: preparation. Identify the project, author, contact path, and outreach state.
- `recording-scheduling`: preparation. Coordinate date/time, Calendly fallback, calendar, and recording details.
- `recording-intake`: preparation. Record or receive the demo, then create the YouTube draft.
- `video-production`: preparation. Edit/review the video, add timecodes/links, and ask the author for review.
- `publication`: after-event. Schedule YouTube for the anchor date/time, confirm playlist state, and notify the author.
- `promotion-follow-up`: after-event, then done. Ask the guest to share/recommend authors and publish or schedule the social announcement.

purpose.body_md:
## Purpose

Create the Monthly Tax Report workflow automatically on the first day of each month so the operations manager can reconcile the previous month, gather proof, upload the accountant package, send the accountant email, and close the workflow from DataOps without reconstructing the process from Trello or scattered docs.

reconciliation.body_md:
## Reconciliation Notes

- The Trello-derived reference in `work-engine/docs/templates.md` listed 8 tasks because Finom and Revolut statement export were combined.
- The DataOps runtime template keeps 9 tasks and preserves all existing refs.
- The split bank-statement refs are stable: `create-bank-statements-finom` and `create-bank-statements-revolut`.
- The accountant upload destination is modeled as the runtime bundle link `Accountant upload/share link`; fixed SOP/reference links remain references or instructions.
